decision, or to impeach the validity of
theirfindings. Com .v. Skeggs,3 Bush
19; Johnson v.Davenport,3 J.J.M. 390;
but iscompetent to sustainthe verdict
and show there was no misconduct . How-
ard v . Com ., 24 R. 612, 69 S. W. 721 ;
Gleason v . Com ., 145 Ky . 128, 140 S. W.
63 , and see 149, Ky . 632.
(32 ) Where the defendant , in passing
near one of the jury during the trial,
said in his hearing,"Don't hang ," it was
an insult to the juror, a contempt of
court and cause for a new trial. Camp-
bell v . Bannister , 79 Ky . 205 , 2 R. 72 ;
and so where, during a recessof the
court, a witness related to the defendant
and the defendantexplainedto the jurya point in the case. Wooldridge v .
White , 20 R. 1144,48 S. W. 1081.
(33) As to correspondence between the
judge and jury , after they had retired
for consultation, concerninga point at
issuein the case,see Goode v. Campbell,
14 Bush 75 ; as to improper statement
by judge to jury to induce them to make
a verdict, see Randolph v . Lampkin , 90
Ky . 551, 12 R. 517, 14 S. W. 538 ; Asher
Lumber Co. v. Lunsford , 17 R. 559, 30
S. W. 968 ; City v. Bestwick , 26 R. 780,
82 S. W. 569 .
(33a) Certain irregularitiesin em-
panelinga jury were held grounds for
reversal of a judgment . L. & N. R. R.
Co. and Curt Jones v. Owens ; Same v .
Parsons , 164 Ky . 557, 175 S. W. 1039.
(33b) In this case the limitation of
an argument to ten minutes was heldreversibleerror. Murphy v. Ray , 161
Ky . 384, 170 S. W. 946.
(33c) A bystander was summoned on
the jury by a name differingfrom hisown . The factthat the party who was
defeated in the action would not have
accepted him had it not been misled by
the misnomer was not ground for a new
trial LouisvilleRy . Co. v. Smock , 157
Ky . 11, 162 S. W. 546.
(33d) The affidavitof a juror is not
competent to prove misconduct of the
jury. Smith's Admx . v . Middlesboro E.
Co., 164 Ky . 46 , 174 S. W. 773.
(33e) In thiscase certainlanguage had
been improperly used by counsel for
plaintiffin argument,and the courtad-
monished the jury to disregard the same .
It was not error for the court to over-
rule defendant's motion to discharge the
jury. Glasgow E. L. & IceCo.v.Clark's
Admx .,158 Ky .734,166 S. W. 214.
(34 ) A party who failed to make de-
fense because the adverse party told him
case was settled is entitled to a new
trial. Head v . Ayer , 24 R. 728, 70 S. W.
55 .
(34a ) The fact that plaintiffhad said
to defendant that he should like to set-
tle the mater out of court was not suf-
ficientreason for a failure on the part
of the defendant to make defense .
Stamper v. Forman -Earle Co., 158 Ку .
324 , 164 S. W. 937 .
(35) Instructions. "Grounds which
merely statethat the courterredin giv-
ing or refusing instructions is sufficient
to raise any and all questions upon this
point. Helfrich v. Everly, 17 R. 795,
32 S. W. 750 ; American Ins.Co. v .Austin ,
18 R. 632,37 S. W. 678 ; Meaux v .Meaux ,
81 Ky . 475, 5 R. 548 ; L. & N. R. R. v .
McCoy , 81 Ky . 403, 5 R. 397; but where
the party expressly indicates the giving
or refusal of certain instructions as error
and names none others, either expressly
or in a general way , he will be confined
to them . Johns v . L. & N. R. R. , 10 R.
757, 10 S. W. 417; Bland v.Gaither, 10
R. 1033,11 S. W. 423 ; and ifno complaint
is made about instructions in motion
they will not be considered. Lawson v .
Lightfoot, 27 R. 217, 84 S. W. 739.
(36) Where one ground for new trial
was that court erred in giving certain
designated instructions, and another
ground was that court erred in giving
an instructionas to compensatory dam-
ages, this latter instruction can be con-sidered on appeal, although it
omitted on firstground . L. & N. R. R.
v .Morris , 14 R. 466, 20 S. W. 539.
was
(36a ) Errors in instructions will be
waived unless objectionsand exceptions
are taken to them in the trial court .
Sword v . Bowen , 157 Ky . 615, 163 S. W.
733 .
(36b) Where no objection was offered
to an instruction at the time it was
given , complaint of it upon appeal can
not be considered. C. & O. Ry . Co. v .
Kelley , 157 Ky . 724, 163 S. W. 1088.
(36c) An instruction not complained
of in the grounds for a new trial can
not be complained of on appeal. Asher v.
Metcalf, 152 Ky .632,153 S. W. 987.
(36d ) A judgment was reversed be-
cause of an erroneous instruction, al-
though the appellantdid not ask a cor-
rect instruction on the same point. L.

§ 340] [Title IXNew Trials .
& E. Ry . Co. v. Crawford, 155 Ky . 723,
160 S. W. 267 .
(36e) Negligenceisa questionfor the
court, where the facts are admitted or
are establishedby undisputedtestimony.
L. & N. R. R. Co. v. Cooper, 164 Ky .
489 ,175 S. W. 1034.
(36f) An instructionrequiringonly
ordinarycare of a streetrailway com-
pany maintainingin the publicstreeta
trolleycarrying a high power of elec-tricitywas erroneous. Owensboro City
R. R. Co. v. Haden ; L., H. & St. L. Ry .
Co. v . Same ,155 Ky .283, 159 S. W. 792.
(37) Motion for new trial- purpose
and effect of. Neither a motion for a
new trialnor billof exceptions is neces-
sary to enablethe unsuccessfulparty to
maintain an appeal. The only officeof
a motion for a new trial and a bill of
exceptions is to bring before the court
for revision errors occurring during the
trial which would not otherwise appear .
When an appeal is prosecuted from a
judgment on a verdict, or the judgment
of the court when the law and facts are
submitted to it, without a motion for a
new trialand a billof exceptions, noth-
ing isbefore the court, except the plead-
ings, verdictand judgment. Harper v.
Harper, 10 Bush 447; McAllisterv. Ins.
Co., 78 Ky . 531, 80 Ky . 176, 3 R. 677,
82 Ky . 678, 6 R. 702 ; Owensboro R. R.
v . Barker , 15 R. 175, 22 S. W. 444 ; nor
is motion for necessarywhen pleadings
are not sufficientto support verdict.
Owensboro R. Co. v. Barker, 15 R. 175,
22 S. W. 444 .
(37a ) An alleged error on a trialcan
not be reviewed unless it be made a
ground fora new trial. L. & N. R. R. Co.
v.Culbertson,159 Ky .561, 165 S. W. 681.
(38) Motion for- suspends judgment .
A motion for a new trial suspends the
judgment, and may be continued and
passed on at the subsequentterm. But
a motion to set aside an order overrul-
ing a motion for a new trial does not
suspend the judgment . There is no au-
thority for a motion to rehear a motion
for a new trial. Louisville R. & L. Co.
*v . Kerr ,78 Ky . 12 ; Harper v .Harper , 10
Bush 447 ; Turner v. Johnson , 18 R. 202,
35 S. W. 923 .
(39) Newly discovered evidence. "The
general rule governing applicationsfor
new trialsupon the ground of newly dis-
covered evidence are : 1. That 'the names
of the witnesses who have been discov-
ered' must appear. 2. That the party
has been vigilantin preparinghis case
for trial. 3. That the new facts were
discovered after the trialand would be
important. 4. That the evidencediscov-
ered will tend to prove facts which were
not directlyin issue on the trialor were
not then known , nor investigatedby the
proof. 5. That the new evidenceis not
merely cumulative." Pricev.Thompson ,
84 Ky . 219 ,8 R. 201, 1 S. W. 408 ; Cahill
v . Mullins , 101 S. W. 336, 31 R. 72 ; L.
C. R. Co. v. Wilson , 103 S. W. 364, 31 R.
793 .
(40) The fact that a party does not
know of the evidenceby which he can
establish his defense does not excuse him
from presenting the defense. A defense
of which the defendant must have had
knowledge , if it was true, not reliedon ,
or presentedon the trial, can not bemade available on a motion for a new
trial. Mason v . Mason ,5 Bush 187.
(41 ) Where a party discovers testi-
mony ,even afterthe argument has com-
menced, and it can then be given, it is
his duty to ask that itbe heard,and if
he fail to offer it, it is no ground for a
new trial; nor can a party get a new
trial on the ground that he has discov-
eredtestimonythatwould strengthenhis
case. Fleet v . Hollenkemp , 13 B. M. 220.
(42 ) Cumulative evidence is not suffi-
cient to authorize a new trial. Mason
v.Mason , 5 Bush 187; Allen v. Perry, 6
Bush 85; Bellv.Offut, 10 Bush 632; nor
isevidencethat only tends to discredit
or impeach an opposing witness. L. &
N. R. R. v.Tinkham ,19 R. 1784,44 S.W.
439 ; L. & N. R. Co. v . Ueltschi, 104 S.
W. 320, 31 R. 933, 126 Ky . 556.
(42a ) The circuit court rightly re-
fusedtogrant a new trialon the ground
of newly discoveredevidence, where this
evidencewas only corroborative. Mul-
loy v.City of Louisville,161Ky .596,171
S. W. 190 .
(42b ) A new trialin a proceeding will
not be granted for merely cumulative
evidencediscreditingdiscreditinga witness. In re
T. A. McDonald , 157 Ky . 92, 162 S. W.
566 .
(43 ) When the ground for a new trial
is newly discovered evidence, affidavit
of newly discovered witness must be filed
to sustain it. Slone v .Slone,2 Met .339 ;
Bowling v . Com ., 148 Ky . 9, 145 S. W.
1126 ; American Central Ins. Co. v . Har-
din , 148 Ky . 246. 146 S. W. 418.
(43a) A judgment was reversed for
failure of the circuit court to grant a
new trialon the ground of newly discov-
ered evidence. Goddard v.Latta ,152 Ky .
538 , 153 S. W. 737 .
(43b ) A new trial on the ground of
newly discovered evidenec will not be
granted unless the evidence be of such a
282

Title IX ) [§ 340New Trials .
characteras to rendera differentresult
reasonablycertain. Weaks v.McDowell
Const. Co. and L. & N. R. Co., 153 Ky .
691, 156 S. W. 127 ; S. Cov . & C. St. Ry .
Co. v . Lee , 153 Ky . 621, 156 S. W. 99 .
(44) To entitlea party to a new trial
on the grounds of newly discoveredevi-
dence, itmust not only have been dis-
coveredtoo lateto have been used upon
the trial, but must have been of such a
nature or so concealed that it could not
have been previouslydiscoveredby the
use of reasonablediligence. The dis-
covery of a deed on record in the proper
officewill not entitle a party to a new
trial. Denny v . Wickliffe, 1 Met . 216 ;
Howton v.Roberts ,20 R. 1331, 49 S. W.
340; nor willthe fact that a party did
not know of a city ordinance. 3 Bush
703 , 7 Bush 308 ; but if the failure to
finda deed or recordisdue to factthat
it is not on index a new trial should be
granted. Elliottv. Harris, 81 Ky . 470,
5 R. 499 ; Hall v. Com ., 17 R. 231, 30 S.
W. 877 .
(45 ) The statement in the affidavitof
the witness filedin support of the mo-
tion for a new trial that he had not
communicated the facts to the party
until after the trial, is not sufficient;
the party himself should state that the
facts were unknown to him until after
the trial. Bronson v. Green , 2 Duv . 234.
(46 ) Where a party loses a case be-
cause of insufficientevidence, he is en-
titledto a new trialifprevailingparty
confessthatverdictisagainstjustice
and truth, and is willingto so testify.
Pigg v. Whitman , 2 R. 320.
(47 ) To entitlea party to a new trial,
when the point upon which it is sought
was in issue in the former trial, the dis-
covered evidence must be of such a per-
manent and unerring character as to
preponderategreatly, or have a decisive
influenceupon the evidence to be over-
turnedby it. Allen v.Perry,6 Bush 85;
Mitchell v. Berry , 1 Met . 602 ; Mercer v .
Mercer , 87 Ky .21,9 R. 870,7 S. W. 307 ;
Porter v. First Nat . Bank , 2 R. 212 ;
Richardson v. Huff, 19 R. 1428, 43 S. W.
454; L. & N. R. R. v. Tinkham , 19 R.
1784,44 S.W. 439;Mercerv.King,19R.781,42 S. W. 106; Ousley v.Ousley, 25
R. 1186,77 S. W. 397, 1117 Ky . 47 ; L. &
N. R. Co. v. Ueltschi, 104 S. W. 320, 31
R. 933, 126 Ky . 556; Torian v. Terrell,
93 S. W. 10, 29 R. 308, 122 Ky . 745 .
(48 ) It is a general rule that a new
trialshould not be granted upon the sole
ground of a discoveryafter verdictof
parol testimonyconcerninga point liti-
gated, or a fact known to a party . Leon-
hart v. Stalzenberger, 7 Bush 209 ; Rice
v . Clark, 25 R. 1060, 76 S. W. 1087;
Finleyv.Curd,22 R. 1912,62 S.W. 501;
Mercer v.Mercer,87 Ky .21, 9 R. 870,7
S. W. 307 .
(49 ) The newly discovered evidence
that will authorize a new trial is not
confinedto the evidence that may be
given by livingwitnesses, but is any
character of evidence that is admissible
to illustratethe point in issue. L. & Ν .
R. R. v.Whitley Co., 100 Ky . 413, 18 R.
868 , 38 S. W. 678 .
(49a ) On an application for a new
trial on the ground of newly discovered
evidence or of misconduct of the jury ,
oral testimony may be heard . Barnes v .
Com ., 179 Ky . 725, 201 S. W. 318.
(49b ) Certain newly discovered evi-
dence justifiedthe granting of a new
trialin an actionagainsta surgeon for
malpractice. Mason,Evans& Keys v.Meloan,165 Ky .582,177 S.W. 435.
(49c) When newly discovered evidence
is of such a permanent and unerring
character as to preponderate greatly, a
new trialwill be granted . Dunn v . Blue
Gr . Realty Co., 163 Ky . 384, 173 S. W.
1122 .
(49d) Under this sectionplaintiffwas
held not entitled to a new trial on the
ground of newly discovered evidence,
where no effort was made to discover the
facts sought to be established. J. D.
Hughes Lumber Co. v.Knuckles,168 Ky .
99, 181 S. W. 935.
(49e ) A motion for a new trial on the
ground of newly discovered evidence
which was practicallydecisiveof the
case should have been sustained. (Sub-
sec. 7.) C., N. O. & T. P. Ry . Co. v .
Cecil, 164 Ky . 377, 175 S. W. 654.
(50) New trial on payment of costs.
The Ky . Stat., sec. 899, provides that a
new trialmay be grantedupon condition
that the costs of former trial are paid
by the party applying thereforwithin
forty days . But the court is vested with
a sound legal discretion in each case,
and may refuse to set aside the order
granting a new trial, although the costs
are not paid within forty days . The
time should not be less than forty days .
Myers v . Lummis , 80 Ky . 456, 4 R. 301 ;
and see Mattingly v . L. & N. R. R., 92
Ky . 463 , 13 R. 666 , 25 S. W. 830 ; Gal-
braith v .Galbraith,5 R. 859.
(51) The court can not order all costs
to be paid. but only costs of former
trial,but when court granted a new trial
upon payment of all costs, the party
should have offered to pay costs of for-
mer trial. Carbon v . Stout , 7 Bush 609 ;

§ 340 ] [TitleIXNew Trials .
and when a new trialisgrantedbecause
of error of the court in instructing the
jury over the objectionof a party, he
should not ,when granted a new trial,be
requiredto pay any costs. Ullman v.
Abraham , 9 Bush 738.
(52) Peremptory instruction. Motion
fornew trialnecessary. Kistlerv.Slaugh-
ter, 20 R. 1937, 50 S. W. 529; Com . v.
Standard OilCo.,112 S. W. 632, 129 Ky .
546; Witt v. L. & E. Ry . Co., 158 Ky .
401 , 165 S. W. 399 .
(53) Practice- the proper method of
bringing to Court of Appeals a case to
correcterrorsof courtduring trialisby
appeal and not by petitionfor a new
trial. Beckham v. Morrison , 14 R. 241,
20 S. W. 197 .
(54) After getting a new trial, the
party obtainingcan not appeal from the
originaljudgment. McCall v.Hitchcock,
9 Bush 66 .
(55 ) Order granting a new trial is a
final order and court has no control over
it after expiration of term . Snyder v .
Cox , 21 R. 796, 53 S. W. 263; Williams
v .Williams , 107 Ky . 496 ; 21 R. 1208, 54
S. W. 716 .
(56 ) Provisional remedies- motion for
new trial not necessary in. Francis v .
Burnett , 84 Ky . 23 , 7 R. 715 ; Crouch v .
The Meguiar-Harris Co., 19 R. 819, 42
S. W. 91 .
(57) Reversal with directions to enter
judgment set aside. The Court of Ap-
peals may , when a new trial has been
granted and thereisan appealfrom the
secondjudgment,directthe entry of first
judgment ifit has been erroneouslyset
aside . Couadeau v . American Accident
Co., 95 Ky . 280, 15 R. 667, 25 S. W. 6 ;
L. & N. R. Co. v. Ricketts, 21 R. 662,
52 S. W. 939 ; Crowley v . L. & N. R. Co.,
21 R. 1434, 55 S. W. 434 ; Brown v. L. &
N. R. Co., 144 Ky . 546, 139 S. W. 782 ;
Ross v.Kohler ,163 Ky .583 ,174 S. W. 36,
and see note 15 .
(57a ) A new trialwas granted and an
appeal was taken from the verdict en-
teredupon the secondtrial. The ground
for sustaining the motion for a new trial
did not appear . The court refused to
reinstate the former verdict. Greenberg
v. Hyman & Oppenheim, 159 Ky . 618,
167 S. W. 914 .
(58 ) Tort . In action for tort against
several,where a joint verdict is returned
a new trialmay be had as to some , and
the full amount of judgment left stand-
ing againstthe others. Loving v. Com .,
103 Ky . 534, 20 R. 229, 45 S. W. 773 ;
Lou . S. R. R. v. Tucker , 105 Ky . 492, 20
R. 1303 , 49 S. W. 314 .
(59) Trial by court--motion for new
trial. In a common law action, where
the law and facts are submitted to the
court, a motion and grounds for a new
trial made within three days are neces-
sary in order to a review by the Appel-
late Court of any allegederrorscom-
mitted during the trial. In the absence
of a motion and grounds for a new trial,
nothing is before the court except the
inquiryas towhether thepleadingsstate
any cause of action or defense, and
whether theevidenceauthorizesthejudg-
ment . Helm v . Coffey, 80 Ky . 176, 3 R.
677; Henderson v. Dupree, 82 Ky . 678,
6 R. 702 ; Harper v. Harper ,10 Bush 447 ;
Jenne v. Matlack , 19 R. 503,41 S. W. 11;
The Albin Co. v. Ellinger, 103 Ky . 240,
19 R. 1886 , 44 S. W. 655 ; and this rule
applies, althoughthe conclusionsof law
and fact are separately stated. 82 Ky .
678 , 6 R. 702; and see notes to sec. 331,
332 .
(60) Verdict not sustained by evi-
dence . Verdict will not be disturbed be-
cause not sustained by the evidence, un-
less it is clearlyand palpably against
the weight of it. Thompson v . Thomp-
son, 93 Ky .435, 14 R. 513, 20 S. W. 373;
Urso v . Unverzagt , 2 R. 228 ; McClain v .
Esham , 17 B. M. 146 ; L. & N. R. R. Co.
v . Graves , 78 Ky . 74 ; Bell v. Keach , 80
Ky . 42 , 3 R. 520 ; Kincaid v . Bull, 159
Ky .527, 167 S. W. 903 ; L. & N. R. R. Co.
v .McArthur ,163 Ky .291. 173 S. W. 770 ;
Stewart & Whitesidesv. Mackin,159Ky .
727 , 169 S. W. 469 ; Denker Transfer Co.
v . Pugh , 162 Ky . 818, 173 S. W. 139;
Adams Express Co. v . Tucker , 161 Ky .
741 , 171 S. W. 428 .
(61) If the plaintifffails to set out a
good cause of action, and the defect is
not cured by answer (Fible v . Caplinger,
13 Β .Μ .464); or if the plaintiffshows
no rightto sue,a verdictfor the plain-
tiffshould be set aside. Petty v .Malier ,
14 В. М. 246 ; and see sec. 386 and notes
theretoas to when judgment may be
rendered against verdict .
(6la ) If a verdict be contrary to the
instructiongiven by the court a new trial
should be granted , whether the instruc-
tions were or were not erroneous . Yel-
low Poplar Lumber Co. v . Bartley , 164
Ky . 763, 176 S. W. 201; St. P. F. & Μ .
Ins.Co. v. Kendle , 163 Ky . 146,173 S. W.
373 .

company for damages resulting from

locomotiveby reason of failureto pro-
vide a proper spark arrester, a verdict

§ 341. (1) Smallness of damages .
When in an actionforany injuryto the
person specialdamages are allegedand
proved, and the jury disregardthe evi-
dence and the law and findforthe plain-
tiffonly one cent,a new trialshouldbe
awarded . This sectiondoes not apply to
the actualpecuniarydamages resulting
directlyfrom the wrong . These dam-
ages can be measured, and the rulethat
a new trial shall not be granted on aс-
count of the smallness of the damages
does not apply. Ray v. Jeffries, 86 Ky .
367, 9 R. 602, 5 S. W. 867; Taylor v.

( la) Where no iterus of specialdam-
age are allegedand proved, but a re-
covery is sought for general damages ,

earnmoney ,thissectionapplieswith full
force,and a new trialwill not be granted
on account of the smallness of the dam-
ages . Rossi v. Jewell JellicoCoal Co.,

(lb) This section does not apply to
actual, pecuniarydamages resultingdi-
rectlyfrom a wrong, which are capable
of being measured , such as special dam-
ages for lost time , medical bills, & c.,
but where such damages are allegedand
proved, and the damages allowedby the
jury arewhollyinadequate,a new trial
willbe granted, Rossi v. Jewell Jellico
Coal Co.,157 Ky . 332,163 S. W. 220.
(2) Plaintiffis not entitled to a new
trialon accountof smallnessof damages
where they cover actual pecuniaryloss

Sears v . L. & N. R. R., 22 R. 152, 56 8 .
W. 725; Lloyd v. Knadler, 22 R. 776,58

for new trial. C. & O. Ry . Co. v . Wil-
liams' Admr ., 179 Ky . 333, 200 S. W.
451 .
(2b) A new trialwillnot be granted
becauseof the smallnessof the damages
in an action in which generaldamages
only are alleged. Conder v.Ledford, 167
Ky . 137, 180 S. W. 77.
(2c) The rule of the Court of Appeals
providing that it will not interferewith
the discretion of the trial court as to
grantingnew trial,unlessthe discretion
was clearly abused ,does no apply where

railroadcompany for death is granted

(3) Three verdictsfor same party.

versed for errors of law , and the third

row , 112 S. W. 639 ; L. & N. R. Co. v .
Daniel,115 S.W. 804, 131 Ky .689; Wall
v .Dimmitt ,141 Ky . 715 , 133 S. W. 768.

a new trialmust be made at the term in which the verdict or decision is

(4) Reversal for other errors may be

cludes all cases in which it is sought to

§ 342. (1) Motion for new trial-
practice . A motion for a new trial must
be made within three days after the
verdictis returned, whether the verdict
be generalor special. ImperialIns. Co.
v. Kiernan , 83 Ky . 468,7 R. 542 ; and al-
though a motion for judgment,notwith-
standingverdict,may be pending. Ruhr-
wein v. Gebhart, 90 Ky . 147, 11 R. 969,
13 S. W. 447 .
(2) The requirement that the motion
shall be made within three days means
three judicialdays, and the day on
which the verdict or decision is ren-
dered , and the day on which the motion
is made , are both computed. Long v.
Hughes , 1 Duv . 387; White v. Crutcher,
1 Bush 472; Humphrey v. Walton , 2
Bush 580 ; Louisville Ry . Co. v . WWell-
ington, 137 Ky . 719 , 128 S. W. 1077.
When judgment was rendered on Jan-
uary 24th, a motion and grounds for a
new trial on January 27th is too late.
Harlan v . Braxdale , 18 R. 171, 35 S. W.
916; Newport News R. R. v . Thomas ,
96 Ky . 613, 16 R. 706, 29 S. W. 437 .
(3 ) When motion is not made in three
days nothing but pleadings and findings
of jury can be considered on appeal .
Western Assurance Co. v . Rector ,85 Ky .
294 , 8 R. 56, 1 S. W. 391 ; Ruhrwein v.
Gebhart , 90 Ky . 147,20 R. 1581, 13 S. W.
447; Seilerv. Gilley Bros., 108 S. W.
284 , 32 R. 1275 .
(3a) This section is mandatory as to
the time within which a motion for a
new trial must be made . Robert's Cot-
ton Oil Co. v. Dodds & Johnston , 163 Ky .
695 , 174 S. W. 485 .
(3b) A motion for a new trial must
be made within three days ; but Sundays
and days on which the court is not in
session must be excluded . The three
days include the day on which the de-
cisionisrenderedand the day on which
the motion for a new trial is entered .
Witt v. L. & E. Ry .Co., 158 Ky .401, 165
S. W. 399 .
(3e) A motion for new trialwas made
five days after the verdict. The evi-
dence on the trial could not be consid-
ered. Johnson v . Boggess , 179 Ky . 649,
201 S. W. 42 .
(4) Motion and grounds must be made
and filedin court, filingin clerk'soffice
not sufficient. Boyle v . Stivers, 109 Ку .
253 , 22 R. 793, 58 S. W. 691 ; Klein v .
Meyers ,24 R. 183,68 S. W. 144.
(5) When grounds for a new trial
have been filedwithin three days after
the verdict, the court may permit addi-
tional grounds to be filedafter the ex-
piration of three days and before motion
is disposed of. Houston v . Kidwell , 83
Ky . 301, 7 R. 201 ; Wooldridge v. White ,
105 Ky . 247. 20 R. 1144. 48 S. W. 1081 ;
Million v . Million, 104 S. W. 768, 31 R.
1156; but additionalgrounds can notbe

§ 343. (1) Grounds relied on - how

davits as to misconduct of counsel in

curred before the court, to which the

term the motion must be supportedby

ing that diligence was exercised to se-

99 , 181 S. W. 935 .

ceedingterm, although motion to set it

cation for a new trial is not an abuse of

dence or of misconductof the jury,oral

and 7.) Barnes v.Com .,179 Ky .725,201

tain ground filedfor a new trial which

countyor twentydays inany othercounty,thedefendantso servedshall

On thenextruleday theoppositepartyshallfilehisreplyand any other
pleahe may have,whetherof law or fact; and so on at eachsucceeding
ruleday untiltheissueshallbe fullymade up. Ifany partyshallfailto
fileany pleaattheruleday atwhich thesame isdue,he may filethesame
at a subsequentruleday ,and as soon as practicableand he shallfilethere-

§ 367a . (1) Practice act- when case

(2) Answer after demurrer . A de-
fendant was permitted to filea demurrer
after the time allowed by law . It was

which set up a defense. It was error to
overrule the motion to file the answer .
Chicago Bonding & Surety Co. v. John-
son, 180 Ky . 589, 203 S. W. 317.
(2a ) Answer , insufficientexcuse for
not filingin time . Time until a certain
rule day was given a defendant to file
an answer setting up his claim. Before
that rule day he lodged his answer with
the clerk of the court to be filed on that
rule day , but did not fileit with the
clerk and give notice thereof. The clerk
failed to filethe answer . A judgment
barring the defendant's claim was af-
firmed. (Subsec . 3.) Kohlman v. Moore ,
175 Ky . 710 , 194 S. W. 933.
(2b ) Removal of cause. A petitionfor
removal of a cause to the federal court

appearance and answer entitled him to

againsttheothers,ifa severaljudgmentbe proper. (When severaljudg-
ments notproper,sec.80 ;and seesecs.363,373.)
Cross reference: See Newman's Pl. and Pr . (3d Ed .), secs. 105, 173d, 329a,438,439 .
been a divorce. A deed made by the wife
in which the husband who was supposed
to have been divorced did not join was
void . Hellard v. Rockcastle Min ., Lum .
& Oil Co., 153 Ky . 259, 154 S. W. 401.
(29) Entry out of term by clerk.
Judgment enteredby clerkout of term
time and without authority of judge is
void. Shepherd v. Shepherd, 107 S. W.
273 , 32 R. 943, 128 Ky . 87.
(30) Judgment conclusiveon parties.
In an action to enforce satisfaction of
judgment ,defensesthat might have been
made when the judgment was rendered
can not be set up. Raymer v. Trustees,
30 R. 332, 98 S. W. 323, 124 Ky . 96.
(31) Collateral and direct attack on
judgment - distinctionbetween . Crown
Real Estate Co. v. Rogers , 117 S. W.
279 , 132 Ky . 790.
(32) A petition for adoption filedin
the Jefferson Circuit Court did not give
the name of the court nor did it state
that the petitioners were residents of
JeffersonCounty. The validityof the
judgment was in nowise affectedby
these omissions. Chester v . Graves , 159
Ky . 244 , 166 S. W. 998.
(33 ) If on a collateralattack the rec-
ord of the judgment attackedshows cer-
tain stepstaken to acquirejurisdiction,
it will not , unless the record be an old
one, be presumed that jurisdictionwas
acquired in some other way . Baker v .
Baker , Eccles & Co., 162 Ky . 683, 173 S.
W. 109 .
(34) A judgment of a court in this
State can not be attacked directlyexcept
by appeal or by the proceedings of secs.
340 , 414 or 518. All other attacks are
collateral. Upon a collateralattack
every presumption must be indulgedin
favor of the judgment. Baker v.Baker,
Eccles & Co., 162 Ky . 683, 173 S. W. 109.
§ 369. Judgment in favor of one or
two parties. Where a railroadcompany
leased its track to another railroad com-
pany and the latterissued forpersonal
injuries,a verdictand judgment in favor
of the lessee is no bar to a prosecution
of the action against the lessor,although
sued jointly. LouisvilleBridge Co. v.
Sieber, 157 Ky . 151, 162 S. W. 804.
§ 370. (1) Continuing action as to
part. In an action in tort against sev-
eral defendants , some of whom have and
some of whom have not been summoned ,
it is error to render judgment against
any of them unless the action is discon-
tinued as to those not summoned on the
firstday of them . Hedger v . Downs , 2
Met . 160 ; Buckles v . Lambert , 4 Met .
330 ; see sec. 363 and notes .
(2 ) Where several are sued and judg-
ment is renderd against some of them ,
it is not neecssary to make an order con-
tinuing the case as to others; the action
as to them stands continued by operation
of law . Patton v . Shanklin , 14 B. М. 15.
(3 ) In an action on a return of "no
property" against the execution defend-
ant and others who are made defendants ,
and who , it is alleged, are each sepa-
rately indebted to the execution defend-
ant, in certainspecifiedamounts, judg-
ment may be rendered against some of
them and the action continued as to the
others. Joyce v. O'Toole, 6 Bush 31.
(4 ) Judgment against trespassers. "A
judgment against one co-trespasser or
wrong -doer will not per se bar a subse-
quent action against a differentdefend-
ant for the same or a differentasporta-
tion or conversion of the same property ."
U. S. Shakers v . Underwood , 11 Bush
265; see further as to judgments in ac-
tions ex delicto, notes to secs. 363 and
373 .
(5) Jointor severaljudgment may be
rendered in action against trespassers.
Alexander v . Humber , 86 Kv . 565, 9 R.
734, 6 S. W. 453 ; Central Pass . Co. v .
Kuhn ,86 Ky . 578, 9 R. 725, 6 S. W. 441;
and notes to sec . 373 .

§ 371] [Title IXJudgment - In General .
§ 371 [400] [Dismissalof actionwithoutprejudice.) An action,or
any causeof action,may be dismissedwithoutprejudiceto a futureac-
tion-
1. [By plaintiff.] By theplaintiff,beforethefinalsubmissionofthe
casetothejury,or tothecourt,ifthetrialbe by thecourt.
2. [By court.) By the court--
a. Iftheplaintifffailtoappearatthetrial.
b. For the want of necessaryparties.
c. On theapplicationof a defendant,iftherebe otherswhom theplain-
tifffailstoprosecutewith diligence.
d. For disobedienceby theplaintiffof an orderconcerningtheproceed-
ingsintheaction. In allothercases,upon thetrialof theaction,thede-
cisionmust be upon the merits. (Upon failureof plaintiffto securecosts,
sec.617 ;seefurtheras todismissal,sec.85.)
Cross reference: See Newman's Pl. and Pr. (3d Ed .),secs.177,190c, 377, 393c, 587.
(6 ) Judgment - how construed. Where
several are sued and some are served
with process,judgment against"the de-
fendants," withoutnaming them ,willbe
understood as a judgment against thedefendants only who are summoned .
Waller v. Martin, 17 B. M. 181; Clark
v .Finnell, 16 В. М. 329.
(7) In an action against several, one
of them being a nominal and unneces-
sary party between whom and the plain-
tiffstherewas no litigation, a judgment
against "the defendants ," without dis-
crimnation, although erroneous, is aclericalmisprision.prision. C., H. & D. R. R.
Co. v . Spratt . 2 Duv . 4 ; see further,
notes to sec . 373 .
(8) Replevying judgment by part of
defendants . Where several persons are
sued and judgment is renderedagainst
some of them and replevied,it does not
bar proceedingsagainstthe others. Bur-
rus v. Anderson , 3 Met . 500 ; Nat . Bank
v .Bryant ,13 Bush 419.
(9) Judgment against part in action
on contract. Crump v . J. I. Case Ma-
chine Co., 123 S. W. 333, 136 Ky . 60.
§ 371. (1) Counter -claim or set - off
not affectedby dismissal of action; but
afterplaintiffhas made motion to dis-
miss it istoo lateto filecounter-claim.
Sec. 372 ; Northwestern Ins. Co. v . Bar-
bour , 95 Ky . 7, 15 R. 394, 23 S. W. 584.
(2) Defect of parties. When there is
a defect of parties the action should be
dismissed without prejudice, unless
within a reasonable time the defect is
cured. Turpin v.Thompson ,2 Met .420;
Carpenterv.Miles,17 B. М. 598.
(3) Dismissed agreed- effectof. When
an actionis dismissedagreed it willbe
a bar to a recovery on the same cause
of action between the same parties. Hib-
ler v. Shipp , 78 Ку . 64.
(4 ) Dismissal without prejudice- effect
of. A dismissalwithout prejudiceleaves
the parties as if no action had been in-
stituted, and when , on issue formed and
proof heard , the court "dismisses the
action without prejudice," it will be pre-
sumed that the case was not disposed
of on its merits. Magill v . Mercantile
Co., 81 Ky . 129, 4 R. 927.
(4a ) An order of a court that a case
be filed away was construed as a dis-
missal of the action without prejudice.
Phillipsv. Arnett, Admr ., 164 Ky . 426,
175 S. W. 660 .
(4b) Dismissal by court. Where a
paragraph of a petitionfordamages for
slanderous words stated the name of one
personwho was presentwhen the words
were published, it was error to dismiss
the action without prejudice for failure
to filea billof particulars, in obedience
to an order of the court to give the
names of other persons who were pres-
ent. McCullom v . Langdon , 165 Ky . 244,
176 S. W. 990 .
(4c) If a paragraph of a petitionset-
ting up a cause of action for slander
failsto state the name of any person in
whose presence the slander was spoken ,
it is competent for the court to require
a billof particularsspecifyingthe names
of the personswho were present. The
failure to supply this bill of particulars
isground for dismissal without prejudice.
McCullom v.Langdon ,165 Ky .244,176 S.
W. 990.
308

Title IX ] [§ 371Judgment - In General .
(5) Dismissal- when not a bar. Dis-
missal of a cross-bill as to one of the
causes of action set up , with an express
reservation as to all others matters , is
no bar to another action for the matters
reserved. Harrow v. Johnson , 3 Met .
578 ; and see notes 17-21 to sec. 368.
(5a) An order dismissed an action on
a forthcomingbond brought on an un-
signed judgment sustainingan attach-
ment ; it was not a bar to an action on
the judgment when signed. Farris v.
Matthew , 149 Ky . 455, 149 S. W. 896.
(5b) Plaintiffin an action on contract
assigneditsclaim for value and on its
motion the action was dismissed settled;
no satisfactionwas made by defendant .
This order was not a bar in an action by
the assigneeto collectthe claim. New
Bell JellicoCo. v. Braznell'sAdmr ., 149
Ky . 418, 149 S. W. 888.
(6) If a trialwent off on a technical
defect,or becausethe debt was not due,
or because the courthad no jurisdiction,
or becauseof thetemporarydisdisability
of the plaintiffto sue, or the like, the
judgment willnot be a bar to a future
action. Birch v.Funk ,2 Met .544; Pep-
perv.Donnelly, 87 Ky .259,10R. 140, 8S. W. 441; Yankey v. Sweeney, 85 Ky .
55,8 R. 944,2 S.W. 559; seenotes17-21,
sec . 368 .
(7) Dismissalofa petitionabsolutely,
when it should have been dismissed with-
out prejudice,willnot bar anotheraction
on the same cause when the plaintiffin
the actiondismissedhad no righrightof
action; the legal effectis the same as if
the petitionhad been dismissedwithout
prejudice. Bracken Co.v.RobertsonCo.,
6 Bush 69;Hiltonv.Hilton, 110 Ky .522,
22 R. 1934,62 S. W. 6.
(8) Where this order was made , " or-
dered that this cause be dismissed at de-
fendant'scost, and leave is given the
plaintiffto withdraw the note sued on
by leaving a copy in the papers," it did
not bar another action on the note . Hib-
lerv. Shipp,78 Ky . 64.
(9) Filing away case for want of
prosecution is not a finalorder or bar to
a subsequent action on the cause. Nickel
v . Fallen, 15 R. 389; 7 R. 758 .
(10) The practice of filingaway cases
is not authorized by the Code , but if a
case has been filedaway with leave to
reinstate, the plaintiffis entitled upon
notice and motion to that effect to have
case redocketed. Whailey v. Myers , 7
R. 758 .
(11) Final order. When an action is
dismissedwithout prejudicebecause the
court has no jurisdictionof the action,
an appeal liesfrom the order. Wood v .
Downing , 110 Ky .656,23 R. 62,62 S. W.
487 .
(12) Final submission to jury is not
made "until all questions of law have
been disposed of by the court, instruc-
tionsand papers pertainingto the case
actuallydeliveredto the jury,and they
are authorized without further interpo-
sitionor control of the court to proceed.
to a judicial examination of the issue
submitted to them ." Vertree v . Ν . Ν .
& . M. V. R. R., 95 Ky . 314, 15 R. 680,25
S. W. 1 .
(13) Misjoinder- where there is a mis-
joinder,the courtmay requiretheplead-
er to elect,and upon hisfailureto do so,
dismiss the objectionablepart of the
pleading without prejudice. Hilton v.
Hilton , 110 Ky . 522, 22 R. 1934, 62 S.
W. 6 .
(14) Party can not dismiss his action
to the prejudiceof otherswho have the
rightto availthemselvesof his plead-
ing, as in an action under act of 1856.
Heldrich v . Silva, 89 Ky . 422, 11 R. 645,
12 S. W. 770 ; Sawyers v. Sanford , 5
Bush 539 ; Baker v . Kinnaird , 94 Kу . 5,
14 R. 695,21 S.W. 237; Roberts v. Phil-
lips, 11 Bush 11; nor can court fileaction
away so as to defeat rights of other
creditors, 5 Bush 539; nor can a party,after commencement of trial, withdraw
a defense that another party has right to
avail himself of. Hearn v . Lander , 11
Bush 669 .
(15) In action for the recovery of
specificpersonal property, where the
plaintiffhas executedbond and obtained
possession of the property, he can not ,
over the objection of the defendant , dis-
miss his actionand retainthe property.
Rogers v. Bradford , 8 Bush 163.
(16) Practice. An action was dis-
missed without prejudice, and the court,
on motion of plaintiff, set aside order
conditioned upon payment of all costs
by him within sixty days ; failingto pay
costs, order dismissing remained in force
and is not bår to another action . Mat-
tingly v . L. & N. R. R. Co., 92 Ky . 463,
13 R. 666 , 25 S. W. 830 .
(17) An action should not be dis-
missed, with or without prejudice, be-
cause the plaintiffneglects or fails to
have a guardian ad litem appointed for
the infant defendants . It is the duty of
the court to see that a guardian ad litem
is appointed , and where the case is sub-
mitted before the appointment of the
guardian the submission should be set

§ 372 ] [Title IXJudgment - In General.
§ 372 [401] [Trialof set-offor counter-claim.] A defendantisen-
titledtoa trialof a set-offorcounter-claim,althoughtheplaintiffdismiss
hisactionor failtoappear. (As tojudgment on,seesec.387.)
Cross reference: See Newman's Pl.and Pr.(3d Ed .),secs.377,500,849.
aside and a guardian appointed . C. &
L. R. R. Co. v. Bowler , 9 Bush 468.
(18) In an actionagainst an admin-
istratorand others on a bond , ifthe affi-
davit and demand requiredby law have
not been made , the action should be dis-
missed without prejudiceas to the ad-
ininistrator, but not as to the others.
Rogers v. Mitchell, 1 Met .22.
(19) In an action for the recovery of
land and damages for its detention, the
plaintiffdismissed his action for dam-
ages up to the filingof the suit. The
averments of the petitiondid not claim
damages after that time , and a judg-
ment for $500 damages was erroneous.
Shean v.Cunningham ,6 Bush 123.
(20) Right to dismiss without preju-
dice at any time before submission be-
longs to the plaintiffabsolutely, and
court has no discretion in the matter .
Northwestern Ins.Co. v.Barbour,95 Ky .
7,15 R. 394,23 S. W. 584; but a plain-
tiffcan not dismiss his petition without
prejudice after an answer , and set-off
or counter-claim has been tendered, al-
though not filed, and thereby deprive
the defendant of the right to a trial of
his set-off. Ringo v.New Farmers ' Bank ,
101 Ky .91,19 R. 91,39 S. W. 701 ; Glenny
GlassCo.v.Taylor, 99 Ky. 24, 17 R.1331,34 S. W. 711 ; and see notes 11 and
19 , and sec . 372 .
(21) Time when motion may be made .
Plaintiffhas a right to dismiss without
prejudice even after motion for peremp-
tory instructionhas been sustained, but
before instructionis given to jury. Ver-
treev. N. N. & B. V. R. R.,95 Ky .314,
15 R. 680, 21 S. W. 1; Wilson v.Dupree,
24 R. 1456,71 S. W. 645 ; Wilson v .Sulli-
van ,112 S. W. 1120 ; and see notes 12,20 .
(21a) A plaintiffhas a right to dis-
miss the action, without prejudice, at
any time beforefinalsubmission. Ray v.
Ellis, 162 Ky . 517, 172 S. W. 951 .
(21b) A causeisfinallysubmittedto ajurywhen allquestionsstionsoflaw havebeen
disposed of by the court and the instruc-
tionsand papers have been deliveredto
the jury and they are authorized to pro-
ceed to a decision. Ohio Valley Elec. R.
Co. v. Lowe , 167 Ky . 132, 180 S. W. 61.
(21c) After the court has verbally in-
dicated its intention to sustain a motion
for a peremptory instruction, and while
the attorney for the plaintiffis discuss-
ing the propriety of such ruling, and be-
foredeliveryof such instructionsto the
jury for the signingthereof, the court
may properlysustainplaintiff'smotion
to dismisswithout prejudice. Ohio Val-
ley Elec. R. Co. v. Lowe , 167 Ky . 132,
180 S. W. 61.
(21d ) Where a motion for a peremp-
tory instructionwas sustained, and the
verdict written out, and the foreman of
the jury directedto sign it, it was too
late for the plaintiffto dismiss the action
without prejudice. These thingsconsti-
tute a final submission . Ills. Cent . Ry .
Co. v. Seibold, 160 Ky . 139, 169 S. W.
610 .
(22 ) Trespassers. Any one or all of
jointtrespassersmay be sued,and a dis-
mission as to, or release of, one or more
who are sued does not release the others .
Sellards v. Zomes ,5 Bush 90 ; Buckles v .
Lambert,4 Met .330. See further,notes
to secs. 370 , 373 .
(23 ) Dismissal for failure to pay cost
iserror. Hobbs v. L.H. & St.L. R. Co.,
102 S. W. 818, 31 R. 452,126 Ky .1 ; Wil-
son v . Sullivan, 112 S. W. 1120 .
(24 ) After submission to the court it
is too late to demand right to dismiss
withoutprejudice, but the courtmayallow it to be done . Welhelm v . Bains ,
147 Ky . 832, 145 S. W. 1125 ; Williamson
v. American Bond Co., 140 Ky . 215, 130
S. W. 1072 .
(25) Court may dismiss case set down
to a day certain for trial although the
attorneys agree on a continuance . Con-
rad Fruit Co. v. Bondurant , 121 S. W.
482, 134 Ky . 568.
(26 ) Striking case from docket with-
out any other order is in effect a dis-
missal without prejudice. Wilson v. De
Loach , 96 S. W. 514, 29 R. 823, 123 Ky .
393; Porter v . Redmond , 96 S. W. 529,
29 R. 846, 123 Ky .400 .
(27) Finality of judgment. Under
subsec. 2 , a trial court may not dismiss
an action absolutely, except upon motion
of the plaintiff,or upon finalhearingon
the merits. Tandy & Farleigh Tob . Co.
v.Briggance & Price, 181 Ky . 13.
§ 372. (1) Set-off- counter-claim . Al-
though the plaintiffmay dismisshispe-
tition,the defendant is entitled to a trial
of his set -off and counter -claim . Bra-

§ 373 [402] [Judgmentmay beagainstpartofdefendants.] Though
severaldefendantsaresummoned ,judgmentmay be renderedagainstany
of them, if theplaintiffwould have been entitledto judgmentsagainst
them inan actionagainstthem alone. (When severaljudgmentsnot al-
lowed, sec.80 ;seefurtheras to when actionsstandfor trial,secs.363,
370,367,367a.)

§ 374 [403] [Enforcementof liennottobe postponed.) In an action
toenforcea mortgageor otherlien,a saleof thepropertymay be ordered
withoutgivingtimetopay money or do otheract. (Proceedingifseveral
lienson same realproperty,sec.694.)
shears v.Letcher Co., 19 R. 478,41 S. W.
22; but thisrightonly existswhere the

in court beforethe plaintiffhas made a

western Ins. Co. v. Barbour, 95 Ky . 7,
15 R. 394,23 S. W. 584; but see Ringo

(la) In an action on a note by an in-
dorsee fraud was pleaded and a cancella-

§ 373. (1) Answer by one of several
obligors. In an actionagainstseveralobligorsona contract,a pleaofpayment
or release, which extinguishesthe de-
mand sued on , inures to the benefit of
all, and no judgment can be rendered
againstany of them untilthe pleaisdis-
posed of. But a personalplea, such as
infancy , non est factum , or coverture,
by one does not prevent judgment from
being taken against the others. Rouse
v .Howard , 1 Duv .31 .
(2) Judgment against one of several
obligors. In an actionupon a jointun-
dertaking , judgment may be rendered
against one or more of the obligors.
Moore v . Estes , 79 Ky . 282 , 2 R. 256 ;
Quisenberry v . Artis, 1 Duv . 30 ; Crump
v . Case Machine Co., 123 S. W. 333, 136
Ky . 60.
(3 ) Partners - judgment against one .
A creditormay , at his election, sue any
or all of the members of a partnership,
and a judgment againstone willnot be
a bar to proceedingsagainstthe others.
Williams v. Rogers, 14 Bush 776; and
see Hunt v. Semonin , 79 Kv . 270, 2 R.
334; Moore v . Estes,79 Ky . 282,2 R. 256.
(4 ) Tort. In actionfortort,theplain-
tiffmay , when allthe defendantshave
been summoned , dismisshis petitionas
to any of them at any time beforetrial,
and demand a trialas to the others. See
Baumeister v. Markham , 101 Ky . 122,
19 R. 308, 39 S. W. 844 ; 41 S. W. 816 ;
construingthissectionand see.363.
are(5 ) Where several trespassers
sued jointly,and a jointverdictreturned,
a new trialmay be granted as to some
of them , and the fullamount of the
judgment left standing against the
others. Loving v. Com ., 103 Ky .534,20
R. 229, 45 S. W. 773 ; and so there may
be a reversal as to some and affirmance
as to others . Louisville S. R. R. v .
Tucker , 105 Ky .492,20 R. 1303,49 S. W.
314; and there may be a recovery against
any one or more of them . Carpenter v.
Laswell ,23 R. 686,63 S. W. 609 ; Pugh v .
C. & O. Ry . Co., 101 Ky . 77, 19 R. 149,
39 S. W. 695 .
§ 374. (1) Mortgage ,securityfor debt
only. A mortgage is a mere security
for the debt , and the mortgagor is the
real owner of the property mortgaged,
and is entitled to the use and profits
thereof. The rentscan only be claimed
by the mortgagee by virtue of a contract,
or when a receiverhas been appointed.

§ 375-376 ] [Title IXJudgment- In General.
§ 375 [404] [Foreclosureof mortgage forbidden.) Foreclosureof a
mortgage isforbidden.
Cross reference: See Newman's Pl. and Pr . (3d Ed .), secs. 155, 176c, 184f.
§ 376 [406] [Enforcementof lienand personaljudgment.] In an ac-
tiontoenforcea mortgageor lien,judgmentmay be renderedforthesale
of thepropertyand fortherecoveryof thedebtagainstthedefendantper-
sonally. (Form ofjudgment,Form 195.)
Cross reference: See Newman's Pl.and Pr.(3d Ed .),secs.25d,392g; Form No. 116.
Woolley v. Holt , 14 Bush 788. See fur-
ther, notes to sec. 299 ; and secs. 692,
694 and notes thereto, as to enforcement
of liens and practiceconcerning.
(2) Sale by mortgagee . Provision in
mortgage permittingsaleof propertyby
mortgagee is void. Wilson v. Aultman ,
91 Ky .299 ,12 R. 881, 15 S. W. 783 ; The
Aultman-TaylorCo.v.Meade,28R.208,
121 Ky .241,89 S. W. 137.
§ 375. (1) Foreclosure defined, "A
proceeding in chancery by which the
mortgagor'srightof redemption of the
mortgaged property is barred forever.
This takes place when the mortgagor has
forfeited his estate by non -payment of
the money due on the mortgage at the
timeappointed,butstillretainstheequity
of redemption ; in such case the mort-
gagee may filea bill callingon the
mortgagor in a court of equity to redeem
his estate presently,or in default thereof
tobe foreverbarredfrom any rightof re-
demption ." Bouvier Law Dict.
(2) Status of mortgagee who under-
takes in violation of this section to
foreclosea mortgage . Aultman & Tay-
lor Co. v.Meade , 89 S. W. 137,28 R. 208,
121 Ky . 241 .
(3) Mortgaged personalty. Where per-
sonal property is sold, the sellerreserv-
ingtitleuntilpaymentofthepurchase-
price, and the transaction is contrued as
a sale passing the titleto the buyer,
with a mortgage back for the purchase-
price, the sellermay , after lawfully
taking possession as provided by the con-
tract, sell the property at a fair public
or private sale, upon adequate notice to
the purchaser. Montenegro -Riehm Music
Co. v.Beuris, 160 Ky . 557, 169 S. W. 896.
§ 376 (1) Judgment in personam and
in rem . Any rule of practiceby which
the assignee of notes secured by lien on
land is prevented from proceeding at the
same time to enforce his personal judg-
ment and judgment in rem should not
be adopted in case where the rights of
the assignee may be jeopardized. Cham-
bers v. Keene , 1 Met . 289. An assignee,
to entitlehimself to recover against the
assignoron the contractof assignment.
must exhaust his remedies, both legal
and equitable; and this section of the
Code does not lessen the responsibility
of the assignee. Rives v . Brown , 81 Ky .
636,5 R.745.SeeCarlislev.Chambers,4 Bush 268 ; 1 Met . 289 .
(2 ) Mortgagee of personal property-
rightsof. "On a mortgage of personal
property, after condition broken. the
mortgagee may take possession of the
mortgaged property, ifhe can peaceably
get it, as authorizedby the common
law,which righthas never been repealed
by any statute." Brown v. Phillips,3Bush 656 ; and see Brookover v . Hurst , 1
Met . 665 ; The Aultman -Taylor Co. v.
Meade , 28 R. 208, 121 Ky . 241, 89 S. W.
137 .
(3) Personal judgment . In an action
to enforce a lien on land personal judg-
ment may be renderedagainstthe de-
fendant , although he has not been sum-
moned in county where action is pend-
ing. Collins v . Park ,93 Ky . 6, 13 R. 905,
18 S. W. 1013; Mitchellv.FidelityTrust
Co.,20 R. 713,47 S. W. 446.
(4 ) Practice. The court may render a
personaljudgmentandreservetherightto laterpass on the questionof a judg-
ment in rem . Woolley v . Miller, 24 R.

§ 417. (1) Purchaserofproperty. An
attorney for the plaintiff, in an action
against a non-resident, who purchases
the property of the non -resident at the
saleunder the judgment, is not a pur-
chaser in good faith; and his voluntary
conveyance of it to his childrendoes not
make them bona fide purchasers, or de-
feat the defendant's right to restoration.
Lusk v. Salter, 1 Bush 311.

the property from the plaintiffbefore

be protected. Kellarv. Stanley, 86 Ky .
240, 9 R. 388, 5 S. W. 477.
(3 ) By "good faith" is meant a pur-
chase not merely for a consideration,but
also without notice to the purchaser of
an adverse claim to the property by
others. Kellar v. Stanley, 6 Ky . 240, 9
R. 388, 5 S. W. 477.

§ 2117. Equity jurisdiction- cause for divorce. A jury shallnot be im-
paneled in any actionfor divorce,alimony or maintenance,but courtshaving
generalequityjurisdictionmay granta divorceforany of thefollowingcauses,
to both husband and wife :
1. To both parties. Such impotency or malformation as preventssexual
intercourse .
2. Living apart without any cohabitationfor fiveconsecutiveyears next
before the application.
To party not in fault. Also to the party not in fault, for the following
causes :
1. Abandonment by one party or the other for one year.
2. Living in adultery with another man or woman .
3. Condemnation for felony in or out of this State.
4. Concealment from the otherparty of any loathsomediseaseexistingat
the time of marriage , or contracting such afterward .
5. Force,duressor fraudinobtainingthe marriage.
6. Uniting with any religioussocietywhose creed and rulesrequirea re-
nunciation of the marriage covenant , or forbid husband and wife from co-
habiting.
To the wife. Also to the wife, when not in likefault, for the following
causes :
1. Confirmed habitof drunkenness on the partof the husband of not less
than one year'sduration,accompaniedwith a wastingof hisestate,and without
any suitableprovision for the maintenance of his wife or children.
2. Habituallybehaving toward her by the husband, for not lessthan six
months , in such cruel and inhuman manner as to indicatea settledaversion to
her, or to destroypermanentlyher peace or happiness.
3. Such cruelbeatingor injury, or attemptat injury, of thewifeby the

husband divorce, dismissing counter-
claim; on appeal the court held that wife
was entitledtoalimony,althoughdivorce
was obtained by husband . Davis v .

may be maintained, although the aban-
donment has not been for one year.

29 S. W. 17 .

a divorce in an action in which she did
not ask alimony , she can not thereafter
sue for alimony . Campbell v. Campbell ,
115 Ky . 656,25 R. 53,74 S. W. 670.
(4) Appeal. Courtof Appealshas no
revisorypower over judgments granting
divorce,even from bed and board. Evans
v .Evans ,93 Ky .510, 14 R. 628, 20 S. W.
605 ; Irvin v .Irvin,96 Ky .318,16 R. 657,
28 S. W. 664 ; 30 S. W. 417 .

tain an action in her own name for di-
vorce or alimony . Snedager v . Kincaid ,

2. [Allegationas towhere divorceexisted.] That thecauseofdivorce

§ 422. (1) Credible witnesses are per-
sons not disqualifiedby mental imbecil-
ity, interestor crime from givingtesti-
mony in a court of justice. Fullerv.
Fuller, 83 Ky . 345, 7 R. 395.
(2 ) Evidence- one witness. In an ac-
tionfora divorceon theground of aban-
donment , or any other charge except
lewdness or adultery, the testimony of
one witness may be regarded as sufficient
to sustain it. Stibbins v. Stibbins, 1
Met . 476. See Ky . Stat ., sec. 2119 , and
notes thereto as to proof required to
sustain charge of adultery or lewdness .
§ 423. (1) Abandonment . In a suit
by the wife for divorce the court can
not on the cross-petition of the husband
grant him a divorceon the ground of
abandonment , unless the abandonment
has continued for a year before the filing
of the cross-petition. Lee v. Lee , 1 Duv .
196; and itmust be allegedand proved
that the abandonment was without fault
of party applyingfordivorce. Epling v.
Epling, 1 Bush 74; Owsley v. Owsley, 1
R. 124 ; Self v. Self, 1 R. 356.
(2 ) The wife is not entitled to a di-
vorce on the ground of abandonment
committed in another State, unless it is
proved that abandonment was a cause
of divorce there. Hick v. Hick , 5 Bush
670 ; Becket v .Becket , 17 В. М. 370.
(3) Witnesses testifying to an aban-
donment should not be allowed to give
theiropinions,but shouldbe requiredto
give the facts and circumstances. Self

of childrenor allotmentin favor of the wife shalldivesteitherpartyof the

§ 168. Warrant- issualby clerk- arrestand bond for appearance. On such
examination,ifthe childappeartobe lessthan threeyearsold,a warrant shall
be issuedby the clerk, directedto the sheriffor any constable, requiringthe
person accused to be arrestedand brought before the county judge of the
county wherein he may be found, who shallrequirehim to enterintobond,
with good security, in a sum to be fixedby such judge, not exceeding two
thousand fivehundred dollars, for his appearancein the county court of the
county inwhich the warrant issued,on the firstday of the next term thereof,
and toperform thejudgment of saidcourt. (Id.,sec.168.)
§ 169. Commitment tojailupon failuretogivebond . If thepersonaccused
shallfailto give the bond requiredof him, the judge shallforthwithcom-
mit him to the jailof the county whence the warrant issued,thereto remain
untilhe enter into the requiredbond, or otherwisebe dischargedby due
processof law. (Id.,sec.169.)
§ 170. Proceedings- continuance- non-appearance- forfeitureof bond.
Should the casebe continuedat any term of the court,the bond may alsobe
continuedby the consent, in open court,of the accusedand hissureties, or a
new bond given; or, on the failureof the accusedto give bond , he shallbe
committed to jail, there to remain until such bond is given, or the cause is
tried. But should the accused failto appear, as requiredby his bond , and
remain to respond to and satisfyany judgment thatmay be renderedagainst
him,thebond forhisappearanceas aforesaidshallbe forfeited,and judgment
rendered thereon; but, if, afterjudgment is rendered againstthe suretyor
sureties, such suretyor sureties, or one of them, shallenterintobond, with
good security, to be approved by the court,conditionedto pay such sums as
may be adjudged against the accused, in such installments as the court may
direct, as hereinafterprovided, then in thatcase the court shallsetasidethe
judgment upon the forfeiture,the costs,includingfifteenper centum upon the
amount of the bond to the county attorney, having been firstpaid by such
surety or sureties. (Id ., sec. 170.)
§ 171. Trial to proceed in absence of accused . If the accused shall failto
appear, the trialof, or other proceedingsin the cause, shall, nevertheless,
proceed as though he were present; and the courtshall,upon the verdictof
thejury,make allsuch ordersas itshalldeem proper, preciselyas though the
accusedwere incourt,or make allsuch ordersas itshalldeem properto carry
out the provisionsof the precedingsection, should the suretyor sureties, or
eitherof them, availhimselfor themselvesof the provisionsthereof. (Id.,
sec.171.)
§ 172. Evidence- childborn in another Stateno cause for dismissal. On
the trialof the cause, the mother of the child,unless she is otherwise incom-
petent,may be a witnessfor allpurposes; and if the partyaccuseddesireit,

evidencemay be adducedby eitherparty. That the childwas deliveredin

born. (Id.,sec.172.)
§ 173. Verdictfor accused--effect- new trial. If the findingof thejury
shallbe in favorof the accused,he shallbe discharged,unlesstherebe a mo-
tion for a new trial, in which case,he shallbe held untilsuch motion be dis-
posed of; and if a new trialis granted, the same course shallbe pursued
toward the accusedas in caseof continuance. (Id.,sec.173.)
§ 174. Judgment againstaccused--payment in installments- forfeitureof
bond. If the findingof the jury be againstthe accused, they shallfindwhat
sum he shallpay per year,and for what number of years; and the court, in
renderingjudgment thereon, shallmake an order for the annual payment in
such installments(monthly, quarterly, or half-yearly) as shallseem best,and
shallalsomake such order for the keeping,maintenanceand educationof the
childasmay be proper; and incaseof forfeitureand judgment thereon,should
the suretyor sureties, or eitherof them , failor refuseto availhimselfor
themselvesof theprovisionshereof,themoney collectedupon thejudgment
upon the forfeiture,afterpayment of thecostsand fifteenper centto thecounty
attorney,shallbe appliedinpayment of the judgment againstthe accused; and
ifany balanceremains afterthe payment of saidjudgment,itshallbe paid to
the custodianof the publicmoney . (Id., sec. 174.)

vent debtor. The accusedwho has failedto executebond beforejudgment,if
he shallbe adjudged to be the fatherof the child,shallthereupon enterinto

§ 428 1465] [Who may instituteaction- necessaryparties. 1. Who
may instituteaction.] A representative,legatee,distributeeor creditorof
a deceasedpersonmay bringan actioninequityforthesettlementof his
estate[providedthatno such suitshallbe broughtby any of theparties
named exceptthepersonalrepresentativeuntiltheexpirationofsixmonths
afterthe qualificationof such representative.]
§ 428. (1) Action to settle estate
may be brought within less than six
months after appointment of personal
representative,and at any time afterhis
qualification. Holland v. Lowe , 101 Ky .
98 , 19 R. 97 , 39 S. W. 834 ; 41 S. W. 9 ;
Maddox v. Williams . 5 R. 696 ; Brand v .
Brand ,109 Ky .721,22 R. 1366,60 S. W.
704; Faukner v . Tucker , 26 R. 1130, 83
S. W. 579; Alderson v. Alderson, 26 R.
1260, 83 S. W. 1129 : Ky . Stat., sec. 3847 .
By Act of 1918 (Sess.Acts,p.657),no
person can bring this action within the
six months except the personal repre-
sentative .--Ed .
(la) An action to settlean intestate's
estate was brought within six months
from his death ; a purchaser from an heir
pending the suit acquired no interest in
the land . as claims against the estate
consumed the property. It does not ap-
pear in the opinionthat the noticeof ac-
tion mentioned in Ky . Stat., sec. 2358a,
was filed. Robertson v. Hines, 154 Ky .
364. 157 S. W. 704 .
(2) When the time in which a settle-
ment shallbe made is fixedby the will,
creditorsbut not heirs or devisees can
institutean action before that time . Lin-
thecum v. Vowels , 26 R. 221, 80 S. W.
1090 .
(3 ) Administrator- action by not fa-
vored unless the personal estate that
comes into his hand is insufficientto pay
the debts ; ifno personal estate has come
into his hands he should not be allowed
to burden the estate with costs and at-
torney fees. Holburn v. Pfanmiller, 114
Ky . 831, 24 R. 1613, 71 S. W. 940 ; Hall
v . Metcalfe , 114 Ky . 886, 24 R. 1660, 72
S. W. 18 ; Thumb v .Gresham ,2 Met .306.
(4 ) Lis pendens created by institution
of action. Alderson v. Alderson , 26 R.
1260, 83 S. W. 1129 ; Ky . Stat ., sec. 2087.
(5 ) Parties. In action to settle an
estate no persons except those mentioned
in this section are necessary or proper
parties. Debtors of the estate are not
necessary or proper parties. Citizens
Nat . Bank v . Boswell , 93 Ky . 92 , 14 R.
17, 19 S. W. 174 ; Hilton v . Hilton, 110
Ky . 522,22 R. 1934, 62 S. W. 6. While
all creditorsare not necessary parties,
they are entitled to be heard , and in
some cases the general creditorsare nec-
essary parties, as where some of the
345

and allpersons having a lienupon or an interestinthe propertyleftby the
decedent,or any part thereof,and the creditorsof the decedent,so far as
known to theplaintiff,must be partiesto theactionas plaintiffsor de-
fendants. (Words in bracketsadded to subsection1 by Act of March — ,

a
creditors are claiming liens upon prop-
erty that the testator has charged with
thepayment of hisgeneraldebts. Payne
v. Johnson, 95 Ky . 175, 15 R. 522, 24 S.
W. 238, 609.
(5a) An action for settlement
brought by a widow of a decedent in
which she did not make his heirs or dis-
tributeespartiesof was properly dis-
missed on specialdemurrer . Hackett v .
StateBank & Tr. Co. and IraScudder,Ex .of the willof B. C. Hackett ,deceased,
and Ira Scudder , Trus ., 155 Ky . 392, 159
S. W. 952 .
(5b ) A statutory guardian of an in-
fant died; in an actionbrought by his
successorand by his personalrepresent-
ative for the purpose of settlinghis
estateand hisaccountsas guardian,and
for the purpose of recoveringfrom his
surety and from a bank that had re-
ceived the proceeds of a certain note, the
amount due the ward , the surety and the
bank and a person who was allegedto
have assisted the deceased guardian to
appropriatefraudulentlyto the guard-
ian's use a portion of those proceeds,
wereproperlymadedefendants. Taylor
v.Harris' Admr ., 164 Ky .654,176 S. W.
168 .
(5c ) In an action to settle a decedent's
estate seeking to sellhis land to pay his
debts , the failure to make the wives of
his heirspartiesis cured, if afterjudg
ment ,saidwivesbecome partiesby plead-
ing,and confirmwhat has been done upon
privy examination , and their right to
compensation for inchoate dower is pre-
served. These steps may be taken after
exceptions to the sale are filed. Barry
v . Fain's Admr ., 172 Ky . 308, 189 S. W.
220 .
(6 ) Attorney fees. Where a creditor
brought suit to settlean estate, but
reallyfor thepurposeof collectinga debt
due him which could have been collected
without a suit for settlement, and when
no service was rendered the estate, he
was not entitledto an attorneyfee out
of the estate. Dougherty v. Cummings ,
20 R. 1948, 50 S. W. 551 ; Hall v . Mt-
calfe, 114 Ky . 886,24 R. 1660, 72 S. w .
18; Holburn v. Pfanmiller, 114 Ky . 831,
24 R. 1613, 71 S. W. 940.
(7 ) A creditorwho brings suit to set-
tle an estate is entitled to an attorney
fee if the suit is one that should have
been brought and was not alone for the
benefitof the creditor. Seibert v.Bloom-
field,23 R. 646,63 S.W. 584.
(8 ) Where attorneys prosecute a suit
for an administrator to recover a claim
they are personallyinterestedin they
should not be allowed a fee against the
estatewhen the heirshad employd their
own attorney. Sims v. Birdsong , 22 R.
1049, 59 S. W. 749; and seeKy . Stat.,
sec . 489 .
(8a) If exceptionsbe filedto a report
of settlement and oral evidence be heard
it should be made a part of the record.
If exceptionbe taken to the attorney's
fee allowed and no evidence be intro-
duced,thequestionof theamount may be
raised on appeal on the face of the rec-
ord. May v.Walter'sExrs.,149 Ky . 749,
149 S. W. 1014 .
(9 ) Right of creditors to contest
claims . In an action for the settlement
of a decedent'sestate, one of the prin-
cipal claims filedwas that of a ward ,
based on a settlementmade inthecounty
court . The other creditors of the dece-
dent had the right to impeach and sur-
charge the settlement made in the county
court, and thereby reduce the claim of
the ward against the estate. Alsop v.
Barbee ,14 B. M. 522 .
(10) Fraudulent conveyance . If the
decedent or his heirs have in fraud of
the rights of creditors conveyed prop-
erty the conveyances may be attacked in
an action to settle the estate . Elliott v .
Scoville, 144 Ку .584, 139 S. W. 806.
(11) Guardian of infantcreditormay
bring suit to settle estate. Beddow v .
Wilson , 90 S. W. 228, 28 R. 661.

§3882 . Choses in action- saleof- compromise of claims. In actionsfor
the settlementof decedents'estates,the courtmay directthe saleof chosesin
action,includingjudgments,and may authorizethe personalrepresentativeto
compromise claimsgrowing out of contractor tortdue the estate,as well as
claimson contractor tortagainstthe estate; and a personalrepresentativemay
compromise and settleany claim or demand for damages growing out of in-
jury to or the death of thedecedent. (Ky .Stat.,sec.3882.)
(12 ) Partial distribution in kind . In
a settlement suit under this action,
where administratrix's answer showed
that thereare no debtsdue by the estate,
itwas held properfor the courtto make
an order directingher to make a partial
distributionby deliveringto plaintiff
certaindesignatedbonds. Stratton v.
Wilson . 168 Ky . 699, 182 S. W. 858.
(13 ) Preference - not allowed to cred-
itor obtaining return of nulla bona . A
creditorof a decedent obtaineda judg-
ament against the administrator and a
return of nulla bona . He could not by
action under this sectionobtain a prefer-
ence over other creditors. Benge's Admr .
v . Creech , 163 Ky . 810, 174 S. W. 517.
(14) Subrogation . An executor will be
subrogated to the rights of creditors
whose debts he has paid. Franzell'sExr .
v . Franzell, 153 Ky . 171, 154 S. W. 912.
(15) Exceptions to sale. An exception
of a purchaserat a judicialsale,that the
depositionson which the judgment for
sale was rendered were taken without
notice, was rightfullyoverruled. Roth-
childv.Wallace, 155 Ky .676, 160 S. W.170 .
(16) Misnomer - amendment . A sher-
iffto whom the estate of a decedent was
committed brought suit to settlethe de-
cedent'sestatestylinghimself"curator.”
Afterward he amended showing the facts.
The salewas sustained. Pierce v. Marrs ,
153 Ky . 748, 156 S. W. 404 .
(17) Judgment- finalityof. In a suit
to settle a decedent's estate an order
confirmingthe reportof a commissioner
which disallowsthe claim isa finaljudg-
ment against such claim, and is a bar to
a subsequent assertion of such a claim
in the same action. Wood v . Sharp's
Admr ., 159 Ky .46, 166 S. W. 787.
(18) In an action to settlea decedent's
estate the circuitcourt may at a term
after judgment has been rendered fixing
the amount of advancements , permit the
filingof amended and supplemental
pleadings for the purpose of procuring
a sale to satisfy the debts. Aylor v .
Aylor,158 Ky .713, 166 S. W. 216.
(19) Void judgment- not curableafter
sale- lunatic. A judgment directing the
sale of land in which a person of un-
sound mind had an interest was void for
failure to serve process on his commit-
tee. This could not be cured by the filing
of a pleading by the committee after a
sale under the judgment . Barry v .Fair's
Admr ., 172 Ky . 308, 189 S. W. 220.
§ 429. (1) Judgment to sellmore land
than necessary. In an action to settle
the estate of a decedent , a judgment or-
dering a sale of the whole tract, when
it is only necessary to sella portion of it
to pay the debts , is void if there are
infant defendants . Only a sufficiencyof
the land to pay the debts should be sold.
Gill v. Given, 4 Met . 197; Auxier v.
Clarke , 26 R. 890, 82 S. W. 605. See
'Thornton v . McGrath . 1 Duv . 349, and
note 16 , sec . 489 .
(2 ) Petition - requisites of. See Hol-
land v . Lowe , 101 Ky . 98, 19 R. 97, 39
S. W. 834 ; 41 S. W. 9 ; Rodgers V.
Rodgers , 17 R. 358, 31 S. W. 139 ; Meyer
v . Zotel, 96 Ky . 362, 16 R. 506, 29 S. W.
28; Faulkner v. Tucker, 26 R. 1130, 83

(2a ) In a suit to settlethe estate of a
decedent a sale of real estate was not
erroneousalthough there was no testi-
mony showing that the deceasedleftno
personal property or that the land was
indivisible. It clearly appeared from

(3) Sale of real estate may be ordered
before an order is made referring case
to a commissioner for proof of claims,
and before there is any report of claims

Harlammert v . Moody , 15 R. 839, 26 S.

(3a) Undivided interest. A sale of an
undivided interest of a decedent in land
for payment of his debts was valid. His
heirs were infants. Auxier v . Auxier ;
Auxier v. Williams Coal Co., 180 Ky .
518 , 203 S. W. 310 .
(3b) Purchase by administrator as
such- effectof. In a suit to settlethe
estate of a deceased testator and the
estate of another decedent the adminis-
trator of said testator bought land of
said testator and paid out of the assets
of testator the share of the heirs of the
other decedent, and took a deed to him-
self as administrator of said testator .
A private sale of this land afterward
by him was void. The deviseesin
this case were infants . Schlickman v .
Dusing, 180 Ky . 506, 203 S. W. 295.
(4) Titlepapers- not requiredto be
filedin proceedingunder chapter. Rod-
gers v.Rodgers,17 R. 358,31 S.W. 139.
(5) Indivisibleproperty. If it is nec-
essary to sellmore property than will
pay the debts and there are infant de-
fendantstheirrightsshouldbe protected
in the manner pointedout in secs. 490-
496 of Code. Foley v.Graham , 110 S.W.
838; Carterv.Crow , 112 S. W. 1098, 130
Ky .41; Foley v.Graham , 110 S. W. 838,
32 R. 762 .
(5a) The homestead of a person in in-
divisible realty attaches for a reason-
abletime to the funds arisingfrom a sale
for indivisibility. Staun v. Proctor,Jr.,
152 Ky .142, 153 S. W. 196.
(6) Sale of land when not necessary to
pay debts is erroneous, Davidson v.
Marcum , 89 S. W. 703,28 R. 562.
(6a ) In an action to sellland of a de-
cedentto pay his debt ifan heirof de-
cedentisan infantdefendanta judgment
directing a sale is erroneous unless it
appear affirmativelyboth by pleading
and evidencethat the decedent'sperson-
alty isnot sufficientto pay hisdebts. A
settlement of decedent's administrator
referred to in the petition as an exhibit,
but not filed until after the sale will not
sustainthejudgment or the sale.Luscher
v. Julian'sAdmr ., 173 Ky . 150, 190 S.
W. 692 .
(7) Proof of averments of petitionis
necessary before land of infants can be
sold. Tabb v. Wortham , 89 S. W. 191,
28 R. 260 ; Carter v. Crow , 112 S. W.
1098, 130 Ky .41 .
(8 ) Proceeds a fund in court. The
proceeds of a judicialsale to enforce a
lien, so long as they are represented by
uncollectedsale bonds payable to the
commissioner who made the sale are a
fund in court, and can not be subjected
to a debt of the lien creditor by an at-
tachment served on the commissioner ;
service must be made on the clerk of the
court. Patton v . Com ., 176 Ky . 180, 195
S. W. 455 .
(9 ) Proceeds of sale of realty of a
decedent for division among his heirs,
which proceedsare representedby un-
collectedsale bonds payable to the com-
missionerwho made the sale,are a fund
in court within CivilCode , sec.207. Bur-
dine v. White , 173 Ky . 158, 190 S. W.
687 .

commenced ,it is in the sound discretion

(2) Duty of creditorto fileclaim.
When a creditor is made defendant to a
suit to settlean estate, and served with
process.he shouldbe compelledto either
setup or abandon his claim; and if he
fails to present it, its recovery against
the administrator,legateesand distribu-
tees is barred. Hood v. Hood , 80 Ky .

(3) When the provisionsof the Code
are substantially complied with , a cred-
itor who has failedto present his claim
until after estate is settled and funds

and distributees,as provided in sec.434 .
Hill v. Mayes , 25 R. 2023, 79 S. W. 276 .
(4 ) Order of reference. As the Code
imperativelyrequiresan order of refer-
ence to be made in all cases for the set-
tlement of estates of deceased persons,
such orders are ministerial, and not ju-
dicial acts. Smith v. Cochran , 7 Bush
548 .
(5) Limitation. Filing claim with the
administratorof the principal,in a suit
to settlehis estate, did not prevent the
statute of limitationfrom running as to
the surety, although the claim was filed
by requestof the surety. Harris v.Mo-
berly,5 Bush 556.
(5a ) Limitation, pleaded by exception.
In an action to settlea decedent'sestate,
the five-year statute of limitations was
pleaded by exceptions filedto the report
of the commissioner allowing a claim of
the administrator. It was sustained.
'The action was brought in 1911 and the
petitionstatedthat the decedentdied in
1906. Johnson's Admr .v .Johnson , Gdn. ,
155 Ky . 9, 159 S. W. 606.
(6 ) Practice regarding presenting of
claims. The usual practicein actions for
the settlement of the estates of deceased
persons is for creditors to present their
claims, verifiedand proved as required
by statute. Upon exceptions to the mas-
ter'sreport, the sufficiencyof the veri-
fication and proof can always be in-
quired into; and in such cases it seems
allowableto investigateand pass upon
the merits of the claim . But a creditor
bas a right to filehis petitionsetting up
his claim, and when it is controverted
he has the right to demand that the
grounds of defense be stated, according
to the rules of pleading, in the form of
a verifiedanswer , or by exception con-
taining the denials or allegations neces-
sary to present a defense. Horner v.Har-
ris, 10 Bush 357 ; Callender v. Callender,
24 R. 1145 , 70 S. W. 844. See Francis v .
Smith , 1 Duv . 121 .
(7 ) Finality of judgment . A commis-
sioner'sreport in an action to settle a
decedent's estate disallowed a claim , An
order confirming this report was a final
judgment against the claim . The circuit
court after the term had no power to
vacate or modify same in the absence of
some of the grounds in Code , sec. 518.
Culver v. Lutz, 171 Ky . 691, 189 S. W.
240 .

§ 433. Failure to present claim . Cred-

§ 434. (1) Action by infant ward
against creditors. An infant ward failed
to present her claim in the settlement
and distribution of the estate of her
guardian, under the act of 1856. After
arriving at age ,she could sue the credit-
ors who had received the estate . and re-
quirethem to contributepro rata to the
payment of her claim. Roberts v. Phil-
lips. 11 Bush 11.
(2) Creditor to present claim bar .
When devisees, as well as creditors, are
made parties, and one of the creditors
holds a claim which , if allowed , would
alter the division of the estate, such
creditorshould be required to present his
claim , or have it barred against legatees
and distributees, as well as the adminis-
trator. Hood v .Hood ,80 Ky .39,3 R. 515 ;
Story v . Story , 18 R. 97 , 35 S. W. 540 ;
Benson v . Simmers , 21 R. 1060 , 53 S. W.
1035 ; Hill v . Mayes , 25 R. 2023 , 79 S.
W. 276 .
(3) Legatee or distributee, who has
received assets to the extent of the cred-
itor'sclaim, may be sued for the whole
amount of claim and required to look to
his co -distributees for contribution . Ru-
bell v . Bushnell , 91 Ky . 251, 12 R. 816,
15 S. W. 520; and see Ky .Stat .,sec.2084 ,
for further provisionsconcerning liability
of heirs .
(4 ) It is error in action against heirs
to rendera jointjudgment againstthem .
The amount each is liablefor should be
ascertainedand judgment renderedthere-
for. Ransdell v . Threlkeld, 4 Bush 347.
(5) It issuggestedin thisopinionthat
this sectionas to the liabilityof legatees
and distributees applies to the case of
one who has received assets of an intes-
tate or testator in another State , if there
has been no administrationin Kentucky .
Bennett v . Miller, 159 Ky . 104, 166 S. W.
805 .
(6 ) It is immaterial to the liabilityof
heirs or devisees that the assets received
by them were lands in another State.
Taylor'sExtrx.v. Jefferson,167Ky .454,
180 S. W. 801 .

§ 435. (1) Refunding bond . It was

default in favor of heirs or distributees
against an administrator, without re-
quiringa refundingbond. Mountjoy v.
Pearce , 4 Met . 98 ; but observe amend-
ment .and see Fleming v .Jones ,12 Bush
503 ; and Ky . Stat..sec. 3843.

lapse of time . Grigsby v . Wilkinson , 9
Bush 91; but see Wilkinson v . Rosser ,
104 S. W. 1019, 31 R. 1264.
§ 436. (1) Injunctiondoes not stop
limitation . Where suit is instituted to

ors are enjoinedas providedin thissec-
tion, the injunction does not stop the

proceedingson claims,but only prescribes
that they shall be prosecuted in that
action. Biggs v. L. & B. R. R. Co., 79

253 .
(2 ) Injunction granted under this sec-
tion did not prevent a ward from bring-
ingan actionagainstestateofcountyjudge to recoverdamages for failureto
take a sufficientbond from guardian .
Com . v. Netherland, 87 Ky . 195, 10 R.
123, 8 S. W. 272.

"Offset" alone is not sufficient. If the
debt is an accountitmust be proved by

§ 438. (1) Parties to action- practice
-attorney fees. In an action to settlea
trust estate, all persons having an in-
terestin the property ,or any part there-
of,conveyed by the deed, and the credit-
ors of the assignee, so far as known , ex-
cept creditorsholding liensupon the
property , should be made parties to the
action, either as plaintiffsor defendants .
Dobyns v. Dobyns ,79 Ky .95, 2 R. 274;
and see as to voluntary assignments ,Ky .
Stat., sec.74 .
(2) Attorney fees costs commission
-practice . See Ky . Nat . Bank v . Louis-
villeBagging Co., 98 Ky . 371, 17 R. 983,
33 S. W. 101; Courier-Journal v. Colum-
bia Fire Ins. Co., 21 R. 1258, 54 S. W.
966; Columbia Trust Co. v. Morgan , 19
R. 1761, 44 S. W. 389, 628 ; 45 S. W. 65 ;
Farmers Bank v . Norton , 19 R. 1773, 44
S. W. 428 .
(3) Action to settleestatemay be in-
stituted at any time by a creditorwith-
out allegingthat assigneeis in default
in any respect. Bohmer v .LouisvilleD.
Bank , 17 R. 16, 30 S. W. 874.
(4 ) Suit by one creditor inures to the
benefitof all. A suitby one creditorun-
der the act of 1856, ifcommenced in time
and prosecuted to a successful termina-
tion, inures to the benefitof allthe cred-
itors, whether they sue or not . Roberts
v.Phillips,11 Bush 11; Heidrichv.Silva,
89 Ky . 422 , 11 R. 645, 12 S. W. 770 ; and
a creditor who has filedhis action in time
can not dismiss it nor can it be filed
away by the court so as to defeat the
claims of other creditors who had filed
their claims and made themselves par-
ties to the action, Sawyers v . Langford ,
5 Bush 539 ; nor can a creditorby refus-
ing to appeal from judgment dismissing
his claim deprive other creditors of the
right to prosecutean appeal. Heidrich
v . Silva, 89 Ky . 422, 11 R. 645, 12 S. W.
770; and see Linthicum v. Fenley, 11
Bush 131.
(4a ) Injunction - to what extent
granted . In an action for settlement of
an invsolvent assignment a creditorwill
not be enjoinedfrom seekingin another
action a personal judgment . The injune-
tion will go to prevent an enforcement of
a lien and to any proceedings affecting
the assignee. DepositBank v.Kirby,175
Ky . 700 , 194 S. W. 929.
(5) Verificationof claims is now re-
quiredby Ky . Stat., secs.90, 1917.
