92 Ky . 206, 13 R. 547, 17 S. W. 353;
Johnson v.Com ., 14 R. 257,20 S. W. 200;
and see Ky . Stat., sec. 1409.
(29) Directness and certainty in stat-
ing offense, see sec. 124 and notes.
(30) Dueling. See Moody v. Com ., 4
Met . 1; Ward v. Com ., 132 Ky . 636 , 116
S. W. 786 ; and Ky . Stat ., sec. 1269.
(31) Embezzlement. Ky . Stat., sec.
1202, and Shelburn v. Com ., 85 Ky .173,
8 R. 832,3 S. W. 7; holdingthat a clerk,
business manager or other agent is not
guilty of embezzlement where money
convertedby him was collectedby him
forhisprincipaland with hisauthority;
and see Com . v.Clifford,96 Ky .4, 16 R.
184, 27 S. W. 811 ; Bailey v . Com ., 130
Ку . 301, 113 S. W. 140 ; Morse v . Com .,
129 Ky .294,111 S. W. 714. Nor is agent
of individual who fails to pay
money to his principalguiltyof embez-
zlement . Com . v. Bull,5 R. 605. Indict-
ment for need not specifythe coin or
number or denomination of money taken .
Jones v. Com ., 13 Bush 356; nor is
amount of money material. Com . v.
Smith , 26 R. 517, 82 S. W. 236.
over
(32) Generally where the agent has
receivedgoods or money to carry, de-
liveror controlfor his principal, unless
the agent parts with the manual pos-
sessionand deliversthe propertyto the
principalor anotherforhim or placesit
in some depositoryto which the prin-
cipal has access, or over which he has
control, he can not be convicted of lar-
ceny for a feloniousappropriationof it,
the offensebeing embezzlement. War-
moth v . Com ., 81 Ky . 133, 4 R. 937 ;
Johnson v. Com ., 5 Bush 430 ; Barclay
v. Breckinridge, 4 Met . 374 ; Snapp v .
Com .,82 Ky . 173, 6 R. 34.
aver
(33) False pretense. An indictment
for obtaining property under false pre-
tenses must what the false
pretenses were and that they related to
some pretended past occurence or exist-
ing fact, and were made for the purpose
of inducingthepartyinjuredto part
with his property ; no statement of any-
thing to take place in future is a false
pretensewithin the meaning of the act.
Com . v. Murphy , 96 Ky . 28, 16 R. 224,
27 S. W. 859; Glackan v. Com ., 3 Met .
232; Com . v.Haughey , 3 Met 224; and
must negative by specialaverment the
matter as to which the alleged falsepre-
tense was made . Com . v . Sanders , 98
Ky .12, 17 R. 544, 32 S. W. 129 ; and aver
that person defrauded relied on repre-
sentations, Bryant v . Com ., 104 Ky . 593,
20 R. 790, 47 S. W. 578; nor can the
prosecution be maintained if the party
defrauded had the means of detection at
hand . Com . v . Grady , 13 Bush 285 ; and
where the false pretense is made in one
State and the money or property ob-
tained in another, the courts of the lat-
terhave jurisdictionof the offense. Com .
v .Van Tuyl ,1 Met . 1; it is not material
whether or not property obtained be-
longed to person alleged to be owner of
it in indictment. Hennessy v. Com. , 88
Ky . 301, 10 R. 823, 11 S. W. 13 ; nor is
value of article obtained material . Jack-
son v. Com ., 86 Ky . 1, 9 R. 265, 4 S. W.
685. Indictment may charge offense in
differentcounts. Saylorv.Čom .,149Ky .
152 .
(34) If one obtains a loan of money
from another by a false statement as to
an existingfact, althoughhe intendsto
repay it, he is guilty; as is one who ,
while in the possessionof money belong-
ing to another, obtainsthe titleby a
falsepretense,itisnot necessaryin lat-
ter case that possession should have been
obtained by false pretense. Com . v .
Schwartz , 92 Ky . 510, 13 R. 929, 18 S.
W. 775, 19 S. W. 189; see Ky . Stat .,
sec. 1208, and Com . v . Moore , 89 Ky .
542, 11 R. 971, 12 S. W. 1066 ; Com . v .
Barnett , 95 Ky . 302, 15 R. 619, 25 S. W.
109; Com . v. Scroggin,22 R. 1338,60 S.
W. 528 ; Com . v . Beckett ,27 R. 265, 84 S.
W. 758; Com . v. Vaughn , 140 Ky . 559,
131 S. W. 396; Smith v. Com ., 141 Kу .
534 , 133 S. W. 228 .
(34a ) The use of the word "getting"
instead of "obtaining" in an indictment
for obtainingmoney under false pre-
tenses does not render the indictment de-
fective. Hale v. Com ., 151 Ky . 639 , 152
S. W. 773 .
(34b ) An indictment for obtaining
money by falsepretensesdescribedthe
money as "the sum of $2.50." This was
a sufficientdescription. Hayes v. Com .,
173 Kv . 188 , 190 S. W. 700 .
(34c) An indictmentunder Ky . Stat.,
sec.1213b, formaking a falsestatement
concerningone'sfinancialconditionmustset out the false statement . Gardner v .
Com ., 164 Ky . 196, 175 S. W. 362.
(35) False swearing . One can not be
punished for falseswearing unless the
false oath was taken on a subject on
which he couldbe legallysworn and be-
forea personlegallyauthorizedto ad-
minister an oath . Kerfoot v . Com ., 89
Ky . 174, 11 R. 391, 12 S. W. 189 ; Com .
v. Kane ,92 Ky . 457 , 13 R. 655, 18 S. W.
7 ; Ford v . Com ., 16 R. 528 , 29 S. W. 446 ;
but see Com . v . Turner ,98 Ky .526, 17 R.
925, 33 S. WW. 88 ; indictment should fol-
698

Title VI ] [§ 122Indictment- Form and Requisites.
low precise words of statute. Com . v .
Taylor, 96 Ky . 394, 16 R. 482, 20 S. W.
138 ; see Ky . Stat..sec. 1174 ; and Goslin
v.Com .,121 Ky .698,28 R. 683,90 S.W.
223 .
(35a ) The omission of the word
"knowingly" in an indictmentfor false
swearing did not vitiatethe indictment .
Fisher v.Com .,152 Ky .411, 153 S. W. 417.
(36) An indictment for false swearing
under the statute will be sufficient if it
allege that the false oath was taken
knowingly and wilfullyon a subjectcon-
cerningwhich the party couldbe legally
sworn, and before a person legallyau-
thorized to administer the oath . It is
not necessarythat the falseoath should
be taken in a matter judiciallypending
at thetime,or concerninga matterma-
terial to any point in issue. Com . v .
Powell ,2 Met . 10 ; and see Com . v . Still,
83 Ky .275,8 R. 208;Richeyv.Com .,81
Ky . 524, 5 R. 543 ; Biggerstaff v. Com .,
11 Bush 169 ; Com . v. Maynard , 91 Ky .
131, 12 R. 710, 15 S. W. 52 ; Spencer v .
Com ., 15 R. 182, 22 S. W. 559 ; Goslin v .
Com ., 121 Ky . 698. 28 R. 683. 90 S. W.
223; Com .v. Schwieters,122 Ky . 847, 93
S. W. 592 , 29 R. 417 .
(37 ) The matter alleged to have been
falsely sworn to must be negatived by
specialaverment in the indictment. Com .
v . Kane , 92 Ky . 457, 13 R. 655, 18 S. W.
7 ; Com . v . Still, 83 Ky . 275, 8 R. 208 ;
Com . v . Porter , 17 R. 554 , 32 S. W. 138 ;
see further,notes to sec. 134.
(37a) False swearing is a felony if
the testimony be given on a subject on
which the accused is required to besworn. Sullivanv. Com., 158 Ky . 536,
165 S. W. 696 .
(38) Forgery . To charge that the ac-
cused "did forge the name of A to an
instrument of writingpurportingto be
the promissory note of M for $380, pay-
able to C fourteen months after date ,
and dated 4th of March , 1872 , with intent
to defraud," is not sufficient; if the in-
dictment had charged that the accused
signed A's name to the instrument with-
out authority,or that he procured some-
body elseto sign it, with the intention
to defraud , it may have been good . The
acts constitutingthe offense must be set
out . Stowers v. Com ., 12 Bush 342 ; in
Barnes v. Com ., 101 Ky .556. 19 R. 803,
41 S. W. 772; itisheld to be necessary
to aver the name of the person or cor-
poration intended to be defrauded or to
state that name is unknown ; and it is
also necessary to charge in an indict-
ment for forging names to a paper that
the names were signed without the au-
thorityof the persons. Com .v.Bowman ,
96 Ky . 40 , 16 R. 222, 27 S. W. 816 ; and
see Mount v . Com ., 1 Duv . 90 ; Com . v .
Harrison , 17 R. 343, 30 S. W. 1009 ; Com .
v. Lemon , 18 R. 480, 37 S. W. 61 ; and
see sec.133,and Ky . Stat., sec. 1185.
(38a ) An indictment for alleged for-
gery in making a fraudulenttabulation
of the votes cast at a Democratic pri-
mary in 1911 failedto allegethat the
accused acted in pursuance of a duty im-
posed on him by the Democratic state
central committee . A demurrer to the
indictmentwas rightlysustained. Com .
v. Anderson, 151 Ky .537,152 S. W. 552.
(38b) Under an indictment for for-
gery , the defendant can not be convicted
of uttering a forged note . Snyder v.
Com ., 9 Ky . Opinions29.
(39) Indictmentallegingthat defend-
ant did "utter and publish" as true a cer-
tain false, "forged and counterfeited"
orderchargedbut a singleoffense. John-
son v . Com ., 90 Ky . 488, 12 R. 442, 14 S.
W. 492 ; but an indictment that charges
both forgery and uttering is bad , Messer
v . Com ., 26 R. 40, 80 S. W. 489. One
may be guiltyof forgeryby signinga
writing in his own name if it be false in
any materialpart and calculatedto in-
duce another to give credit to it as gen-
uine. Com .v.Wilson, 89 Ky . 157, 11 R.
375, 12 S. W. 264 ; Com . v . Brewer , 113
Ky . 217, 24 R. 72, 67 S. W. 994.
(40) Indictmentfor utteringa forged
writing must state the acts constituting
the utteringand not the forgingof the
writing . An allegation in the indict-
ment that defendant knew the writing
to be false embraces the charge that it
was so in fact. Lockard v. Com ., 87 Ky .
201 , 10 R. 102 , 8 S. W. 266 ; Purvis v .
Com ., 13 R. 744, 18 S. W. 357 ; see fur-
ther as to forgery of bank check, Raw-
lins v . Com .. 7 R. 595 ; Kennedy v . Com .,
2 Met . 36 ; Com . v. Lee , 18 R. 484, 37 S.
W. 72 ; as to alteration or erasure of
writing, Com . v. Walls , 27 R. 739, 86 S.
W. 684 ; Com . v. Hide , 94 Ky . 517, 15 R.
264, 23 S. W. 195 ; as to forgery of wit-
ness certificate, Moore v. Com ., 92 Ky .
630, 13 R. 738, 18 S. W. 833.
(41) Former convictions. Necessary
allegations in indictment charging for-
mer convictions. Boggs v. Com ., 9 R.
342; Mount v. Com ., 2 Duv . 93 ; Taylor
v . Com ., 3 R. 783 ; Stewart v .Com ., 2 R.
386; Chenowith v . Com ., 11 R. 563 , 12
S. W. 585 ; Sweeney v. Com ., 18 R. 1020,
39 S. W. 22 ; Oliver v . Com ., 113 Ky .
228 ,24 R. 84, 67 S. W. 983 ; and see Ky .
Stat ., sec. 1130.

§ 122] [Title VIIndictment- Form and Requisites.
(42) Gaming . An indictment charg-
ing the defendant with permitting"a
game of chancecalledpigeon-hole" to be
played for"greenbacks" in his house is
good on demurrer. Com . v. Branham , 3
Bush 1 .
(43 ) An indictment for setting up and
keeping a keno tablemust aver that itis a contrivance ordinarily used for
gaming . Com . v. Monarch , 6 Bush 298;
and see Ritte v. Com ., 18 B. M. 35 ; Ку .
Stat., sec. 1960; see as to indictment for
permitting slot machine on premises.
Com . v. Schatzman , 26 R. 508, 82 S. W.
238; and permittingminor to play on
pool table. Com .v. Willis,26 R. 515,82
S. W. 236 .
(44) In an indictmentfor settingup,
exhibiting and keeping a faro bank , it is
sufficientto allegethat it was set up , ex-
hibited and kept , without adding any
words descriptive or explanatory of the
purposeforwhich itwas or may be used,
the faro bank being mentioned in the
statute by name . Waddell v . Com ., 84
Ky . 276, 8 R. 249, 1 S. W. 480 ; Com . v.
Monarch , 6 Bush 298; Millerv. Com .,
25 R. 1236,77 S. W. 682; Com .v.Estes,
121 S. W. 423 ; Ky . Stat ., sec. 1960.
(45) An indictmentchargingthat de-
fendant suffered certain named persons
"to play in a house or on premisesinthe
countyaforesaid,thenintheoccupation
and under the controlof the saidPerrigo,
a game of cards,at which game of cards,
played as aforesaid, money or property
was won and lost," is not sufficient,be-
ing too indefinite. Com . v. Perrigo, 3
Met . 5; Com . v. Schatzman, 26 R. 508,
82 S. W. 238 .
(46 ) An indictment charging that the
defendant "was the owner , occupier and
controller of a house and ten-pin alley
at which games were played and money
and other thingsbet, won and lost by
his permission, and that he and others
played at such games , and money and
other valuablethingswere bet, lostand
won thereon," is sufficient. Com . v .
Fraize, 5 Bush 325.
(47 ) A corporation may be indicted
for permitting gaming on its premises .
Com . v. Pulaski A. and M. Association,
92 Ky . 197, 13 R. 468, 17 S. W. 442.
(48) Indictment charging that defend-
ant did "unlawfullyset up, exhibitand
keep for himself and another" a faro
bank , charges but single offense.
Vowells v. Com ., 84 Ky . 52, 8 R. 74 ; see
Ky . Stat., sec. 1960.
a
(48a ) An indictment for suffering
gaming on premises followed the lan-
guage of Ky . Stat., sec. 1978; itwas suf-
ficient. LancasterHotel Co. v.Com .,149
Ky . 443 , 149 S. W. 942.
(48b ) Hog stealing. An indictment
which charged the defendant with steal-
inghogs belongingto John R. Perry and
others, and which did not charge that
thehogs were jointproperty,was bad on
demurrer. Stephensv.Com .,164Ky .265,
175 S. W. 353 .
(48c) Homicide . A certain indictment
for homicide was sufficient. Rose v .
Com ., 156 Ky . 817, 162 S. W. 107.
(49 ) Housebreaking . See Ky . Stat ..
sec. 1162 ; and Com . v . Ward , 14 Bush
233, where it is held that indictment
must charge that the taking was with
the intentionto deprivehe owner of his
propery or to convert it to the use ofthe accused; but in Com . v. Wicker, 9
R. 474, 5 S. W. 428, the court say it is
not necessaryto allegethe name of the
owner of the property taken , nor to aver
that it was taken without the owner's
consent and with the intention to con-
vert it to his own use; to the same
effectis Mitchell v. Com ., 88 Ky . 349, 10
R. 910, 11 S. W. 209; where Com . v .
Ward , 14 Bush 233,is distinguished; and
Johnson v . Com ., 87 Ky . 189, 10 R. 100 ,
7 S. W. 927 ; holding that it is not neces-
sary to describe the owner of the house ,
or property taken with any more cer-
taintythan is necessaryto informac-
cused of the offensecharged, and bar a
secondprosecutiontherefor. See further,
Com . v. Bruce , 79 Ky . 560, 3 R. 366 ;
Farrisv. Com ., 90 Ky . 637, 12 R. 592,
14 S. W. 681; but to constitute offense
there must be a breaking . Webb v .Com .,
87 Ky . 129, 9 R. 1007, 7 S. W. 899 ; and
an aider and abettor may be convicted.
Mullins v . Com ., 14 R. 569. 20 S. W.
1035. An indictment for breaking an
outhouse should charge that it "belonged
to or was used with the dwelling house ,”
Dunn v . Com ., 27 R. 113, 84 S. W. 321 ;
and that the property stolenand taken
away was of value. Polin v . Com ., 19 R.
453 , 40 S. W. 927 .
(50 ) Infanticide.An indictment charg-
ing that the defendant"did feloniously
conceal the birth of a bastard child, the
issueof her body, by secretingthe said
child so that it might not be known
whether or not it had been born alive ,"
isnot good; the acts showing what the
defendant did should have been set out .
Foster v. Com ., 12 Bush 373 ; and see
Com . v. Hopkins , 9 R. 432, 5 S. W. 392 ;
Wade v. Com ., 1 R. 408 ; Frey v . Com .,
83 Ky . 190,7 R. 174; Clark v.Com ., 111
Ку . 443, 23 R. 1029, 63 S. W. 740 ; and
Ky . Stat ., sec. 1220.
700

Title VI ] [§ 122Indictment- Form and Requisites.
(51) Injury to property. Sufficiencyof
indictment under secs. 1256, 1257, Ку .
Stat.; Ellisv.Com .,78 Ky . 130; Com . v.
Powell , 8 Bush 7 ; Com . v . Searls, 3 R.
394 .
(52) Joint indictmentagainstseveral.
If itappear in proof that one is guilty,
he may be punished the same as if in-
dicted alone. Weatherford v. Com ., 10
Bush 196 .
(53 ) Larceny . An indictment charg-
ing the defendant with feloniously steal-
ing, taking and carrying away "one
twenty-dollar note and one ten-dollar
note, both of saidnotesbeing of theUnited Statescurrencycommonly called
greenbacks . Said notes are not and were
not beforethe grand jury,and couldnot
by said jury be had before it, because
the whereabouts of said notes was not
and could not be known to said jury;
therefore, said notes can not be more
particularlydescribed,eitheras to their
date , number . or as to whether they be-
longed to the class technically called
green or black backs ," is sufficientunder
sec. 135. Jones v. Com ., 13 Bush 356 ;
Com . v . Mann , 12 R. 477, 14 S. W. 685;
Bailey v . Com ., 22 R. 512, 58 S. W. 425 ;
Ky . Stat., sec. 1194.
(54 ) An allegation that the property
stolen was "the property of the Tennes-
seeRiver Packet Company ,D. W. Swan ,
Little Brothers and others," is sufficient
as to the ownership of the property.
Reed v .Com .,7 Bush 641 ; and see Porter
v .Com .,22 R. 1657,61 S. W. 16 ; and see.
128 and notes .
(55 ) As to what constitutes larceny,
see Elliottv. Com .,12 Bush 176 ; Nichols
v . Com ., 78 Ky . 180 ; Williams v . Com .,
78 Ky . 93 ; Miller v. Com ., 78 Ky . 15 ;
Warmouth v.Com .,81 Kv . 133,4 R. 937 ;
Snapp v . Com .,82 Ky . 173, 6 R. 34 ; Ky .
Stat .,sec.1194.
(56 ) Chandeliers attached to the free-
hold are held to be the subject of lar-
ceny, and it is immaterial whether the
carrying away was immediate and con-
tinuous , or the removal was at different
periods after severance. Smith v . Com .,
14 Bush 31 .
(57) Local option. "A person may in
the same indictment be charged with
more than one violation of the local op-
tion law ,as it is called; but each offense
should be separately charged , and the
statement of the particularcircum-
stances of each should be direct and
certain." South v. Com ., 79 Kv . 493, 3
R. 276 ; and see Cockerell v . Com ., 115
Ky . 296, 24 R. 2149, 73 S. W. 760 ; Jones
v. Com ., 104 Ky . 468, 20 R. 651, 47 S.
W. 328 ; indicating how indictment
should be drawn ; and Ky . Stats., sec.
2257 ,2557a ,b .
(57a) An indictment for the offense of
delivering in local option territorygoods
brought by a carrierfrom anotherstate
did not show that the liquorwas in-
tended to be received or used in violation
ofany law ofKentucky. The indictment
was insufficient. Adams Exp . Co. V.
Com ., 154 Ky . 462, 157 S. W. 908.
(58) Physician indictment against
under local option law . Com . v . Green ,
80 Ky . 178, 3 R. 659 ; Com . v. Minor , 88
Ky .422, 10 R. 1008, 11 S. W. 472 ; Sarrlls
v . Com ., 83 Ky . 327, 7 R. 473 .
(59 ) Lottery . As to indictments for
sellinglottery tickets, see Com . v. Bier-
man , 13 Bush 345 ; Com . v . Bull, 13 Bush
656. For promoting and aiding the pro-
motion of a lottery, see Miller v. Com .,
13 Bush 731 ; and Ky . Stat ., sec. 2573.
(59a ) Morphine . An indictment for
illegalsale of morphine , under the act
of 1912, Ky . Stat., sec. 3766c, is not de-
fectivefor failureto state that morphine
is a derivative of opium . Com . v . Gab-
hart, 160 Ky . 32, 169 S. W. 514.
(60) Murder . An indictment charging
murder in the following language , to-
wit : " The said William White hereto-
fore, to-wit : on the day of April ,
1871, in the Commonwealth and county
aforesaid, did unlawfully, wilfully, ma-
liciously, feloniouslyand of his malice
aforethought, killand murder his wife,
one Nancy White ," is insufficient,in that
it fails to state the acts showing how
offense was committed . White v . Com .,
9 Bush 178. Where the indictment
charges that the defendant did "killand
murder " it is not necessary to add that
the deceased died within a year and a
day . Jane v. Com ., 3 Met . 18 ; and see
Alford v. Com ., 84 Ky . 623, 8 R. 550, 2
S. W. 234. See Ky . Stat., sec. 1149.(60a ) A certain indictment for willful
murder was sufficient. Greer v. Com ., 164
Ky . 396, 175 S. W. 665.
(60b) It is no objection to an indict-
ment charging two persons with murder
by shooting with a pistol and cutting
with a knife that the particularacts con-
stituting the offense were not severally
charged against each defendant , it being
claimed that one of them did the shoot-
ing, and the other the cutting. Mac-
Gehee v. Com ., 181 Ky . 422 .
(61 ) An indictment for a common law
offense (murder ) should charge that the
act was done "feloniously," or "with a
felonious intent." Kaelin v . Com ., 84
Ky . 354, 8 R. 293, 1 S. W. 594 ; Stroud
701

§ 122 ] [Title VIIndictment - Form and Requisites.
v . Com ., 14 R. 179, 19 S. W. 976. Where
the indictment charges that the defend-
ant shot the deceased, inflictingwounds
from which death resulted, it is not
necessary to aver that the pistol with
which the shooting was done was a
deadly weapon , or that it was at the
time loaded with a leaden ball or other
hard substance. Jeffriesv. Com ., 84 Ky .
237 ,8 R. 237, 1 S. W. 442 ; as to variance
between indictment and evidence, see
Helmerking v. Com ., 100 Ky . 74, 18 R.
576 , 37 S. W. 264 .
(62 ) An indictment in which the of-
fense charged is murder is good , al-
though the particularcircumstancesof
the crime as set out in the indictment
constitute an accessory before the fact
and not a principal. Stricklinv. Com .,
83 Ky .566,7 R. 627. See further,Hocker
v .Com ., 111 S. W. 676.
(63) An indictment charging that
" The said Matt F. Lightfoot, in the
county aforesaid, did wilfully, feloni-
ouslyand with maliceaforethought,kill
and mürder Joseph McDermott , with a
pistol, loaded with powder and leaden
ball and other such substance, which he,
the said Lightfoot, then and thereheld
in his hands ," is not aptly drawn , as it
does not appear whether the accused
shot the deceased or beat him over the
head with the pistol, but there being no
demurrer it was held sufficient after ver-
dict. Lightfootv. Com ., 80 Ky . 516, 4
R. 463 .
(64) Nuisance . As to indictment for
nuisance, see C. R. Co. v. Com ., 80 Ky .
137, 3 R. 639 ; P. & E. R. R. Co. v . Com .,
80 Ky . 147, 3 R. 650 ; L., C. & L. R. R.
Co. v. Com ., 80 Ky . 143, 3 R. 644 ; Com .
v. T. J. Megibben Co., 101 Ky . 195, 19
R. 291,40 S. W. 694 ; Com . v .C. B. Cook
Co., 102 Ky . 288 , 19 R. 1336 ; 43 S. W.
400; as to indictment for disorderly.
house , see Com . v . Bessler, 97 Ky . 498,
17 R. 357,30 S. W. 1012 ; Cheek v. Com .,
100 Ky . 1, 18 R. 515, 37 S. W. 152; Ehr-
lickv .Com .,125 Ky . 742, 102 S. W. 289,
31 R. 320 ; L. C. R. Co. v . Com ., 96 S. W.
467 , 29 R. 750 ; Com . v . C. & O. R. Co.,
128 Ky . 749 , 110 S. W. 253 ; Com . v . C.,
Ν .Ο . & T. P. Ry .Co., 112 S. W. 613 ; In-
dian Refining Co. v . Com ., 117 S. W. 274.
(64a ) An indictment against a railroad
company for a nuisance failed to show
that the things complained of were un-
necessarily done . The indictment was
bad on demurrer . L. & N. R. R. Co. v .
Com ., 158 Ky .773,166 S. W. 237.
(64b ) An indictment charging a nui-
sance against a railroad company main-
tained where a public road crosses the
railroadtracks was sufficientas itclearly
showed that the nuisance was within the
defendant's right-of-way . L. & N. R.
Co. v . Com ., 149 Ky . 459, 149 S. W. 898.
(65) Peddling . See Com . v . Smith , 6
Bush 303; Monk v. Com ., 6 Bush 397;
Com . v . Dudley , 3 Met . 221 ; and Ky .
Stat ., sec.4215 ; peddlers of patent rights
can not be required to pay a licensetax.
Com . v.Petty,96 Ky .452, 16 R. 488,29
S. W. 291 .
(66 ) Perjury . Ky . Stat ., sec. 1173, and
Com . v. Maynard , 91 Ky . 131, 12 R. 710,
15 S. W. 52; holdingthat perjuryis the
wilful giving under oath in a judicial
proceedingor in the courseof justiceof
false testimony material to the issue or
point of inquiry; and indictmentmust
negativeby specialaverment the mat-
ter alleged to have been sworn and ex-
pressly aver that itwas material or state
factsso showing, and also charge that
defendant was sworn by one authorized
to administer an oath ; and see Com . v .
Powell , 2 Met .10 ; Dowdy v . Com ., 13 R.
350, 17 S. W. 187 ; Com . v . Compton , 18
K. 479, 36 S. W. 1116 ; Com . v . Still, 83
Ky . 275, 8 R. 208 , and see sec. 134 and
notes. Com . v. Miles, 140 Ky . 577, 131
S. W. 385 ; Stamper v . Com ., 100 S. W.
286, 30 R. 994 ; and see notes 35,36.
(66a ) Pool room . A certain indict-
ment for operating a pool room was held
to be sufficient. Com . v. Starr, 160 Ку .
260, 169 S. W. 743.
(66b ) Detaining a woman . An indict-
ment that fails to charge that the ac-
cused feloniously detained the female
against her will for the purpose , or with
the intent, of having carnal knowledge
of her , is not good . Wilder v. Com ., 81
Ky .591,5 R. 635; Evans v.Com .,79 Ky .
414, 3 R. 30 .
(67) Rape . See Blair v. Com ..7 Bush
227; Kesslerv. Com ., 12 Bush 18; Fen-
ston v .Com. ,82 Ky .549 ,7 R. 55 ; Bethel
v . Com ., 80 Ky . 526, 4 R. 487 ; Com . v .
Lowe , 116 Ky . 335, 25 R. 534, 76 S ..W.
119 ; Reed v. Com ., 25 R. 1029, 76 S. W.
838; Com . v . Landis , 112 S. W. 581, 129
Ky . 445 ; Jones v. Com ., 124 Ky . 26 , 30
R. 288. 97 S. W. 1118 ; and Ky . Stat .,
sec . 1152 .
(68) Receiving illegalvote. See Com .
v. Gray , 2 Duv . 373, and Ky . Stat., sec.
1583 .
(68a ) Receiving stolen goods . If an
indictment for receiving stolen goods ,
fails to describe the goods sufficiently,
the indictment will be bad on demurrer ;
but this is no ground for an arrest of
judgment. Duncan v.Com ., 165 Ky .247,
176 S. W. 984 .
702

Title VI ] [§ 122Indictment- Form and Requisites.
(68b ) In framing an indictment under
Ky . Stat., sec. 1199, for knowingly re-
ceiving stolengoods, a general charge
that the goods had been stolen before
the defendant received them is sufficient.
A general descriptionof the property
originallystolenissufficient. An allega-
tion in the indictment that the goods
were originallystolenfrom the O. W.
Company is a sufficientallegation of
ownership by that company . Newton v.
Com ., 158 Ky . 4, 164 S. W. 108.
(69) Robbery . An indictment charg-
ing the defendant with having feloni-
ously taken a mare ,the propertyof Wil-
liam Rayburn , "in his presence, and
againsthiswill,by puttinghim infear
of some immediate injury to his person"
is sufficient. Com . v . Brooks ,1 Duν . 150 ;
Breckinridge v. Com ., 97 Ky . 267, 17 R.
163,30 S. W. 634 ; and see Ward v .Com .,
14 Bush 233 ; Dickerson v . Com ., 2 Bush
1: Taylor v. Com ., 3 Bush 508 ; Lisle v .
Com ., 82 Ky . 250, 6 R. 229; Ky . Stat.,
sec. 1159; larceny is a degree of the
offense. Com . v. Prewitt . 82 Ky . 240, 6
R. 195 ; as is an assault to rob . See
Barnard v . Com ., 94 Ky . 285, 15 R. 51,
22 S. W. 219 ; Brown v. Com ., 135 Ky .
635 , 117 S. W. 281 .
(70 ) Seduction as to requisitesof in-
dictment for, see Davis v . Com ., 98 Ky .
708 , 17 R. 1265, 34 S. W. 699 ; Com . v .
McNutt,133Ky .702,118S.W. 979; Ky .
Stat ., sec . 1214 .
(70a ) No new indictment is necessary
where a defendant in a prosecution under
Ky . Stat ., sec. 1214, has married the
prosecuting witness and abandoned her
within three years. The Commonwealth ,
however , may dismiss the original in-
dictment and reindict. Millerv. Com .,
154 Ky . 201, 157 S. W. 373.
(71) Selling liquor. An indictment
against a merchant for selling liquor
without license must state to whom the
sale was made . Wilson v . Com ., 14 Bush
159. See Com . v. White , 18 B. M. 492.
An indictment for selling liquor to an
inebriate must allege that the defendant
knew the party was an inebriate. Com .
v .Bell,14 Bush 433 ; Ky . Stat., sec. 1307 ;
and seeCom .v.Ewing ,7 Bush 105; Com .
v.Davis ,12 Bush 240 ; Megowan v .Com ..
2 Met . 3 ; and see Ky . Stat ., sec. 1307.
For sellingliquorto a minor. See Ky .
Stat ., sec. 1306, and Com . v. Hadcraft ,
6 Bush 91 .
(7la ) In an indictment for the unlaw-
ful sale of malt liquor to an infant with-
out the written consent of the father, it
is not necessary to aver that the infant
is not the child of the accused . Ran v .
Com ., 9 Ky . Opinions10.
(72 ) Indictment for selling"spirituous,
vinous or malt liquor" isbad ,"or" should
be "and." Raubold v.Com .,111 Ky .433,
23 R. 735 , 63 S. W. 781 .
(73 ) Several counts- indictment may
contain several counts and in one charge
accused as principalsand in others as
aiders and abettors. Cupp v . Com ., 87
Ky . 35, 9 R. 877, 7 S. W. 405 ; Gambrell
v . Com ., 23 R. 502, 63 S. W. 272 ; and
note 92 .
(73a ) Several counts- failure to name
county in one of them . An indictment
was in two counts, of which the first
chargedthe accusedas principaland the
second count as accessory. The first
count stated the county in which the of-
fense was committed . The fact that
therewas no statementof the county in
he second count did not in any way viti-
ate the indictment. Hollin v. Com ., 163
Ky .392,173S.W. 1106.
(74 ) Statutory offenses. Sufficiencyof
indictment for, see sec. 136 and notes.
(75 ) Sunday - for working on . See
requisitesof petition in penal action to
recover penalty for. Com . v . L. & N. R.
R., 80 Ky . 291, 3 R. 788 ; L. & N. R. R.
v . Com ., 92 Ky . 114, 13 R. 439, 17 S. W.
274 .
(76 ) Tavern . An indictment against a
tavern-keeperforfailingto setup in apublic room a listof rates must allege
when the rates were fixedby the county
court, or that they had been fixed one
month before the offense was committed .
Jackson v . Com .,7 Bush 99.
(76a ) The words in an indictment "be-
ing a tavern -keeper " do not amount to
a charge that the defendant was a li-
censed tavern-keeper. Com . v . Cooney , 9
Ky . Opinions 1; Com . v . Brooks , 9 Ky .
Opinions 1.
(77 ) Time - allegationas to an indict-
ment ; see sec. 129 and notes.
(78) Trespass. An indictmentprop-
erly describingthe offensedenounced in
sec. 7, art. 28, chap . 39, Gen. Stat . (now
Ky . Stat ., sec. 1256 ), but terming it an
indictment for " malicious mischief " in-
stead of "wilful trespass," is good . Com .
v . Smith , 6 Bush 263 .
(79) An indictment charging that
the defendant"unlawfullyand without
felonious intent destroyed , injured and
carried away timber from four trees on
the premises of John W. Wash , which
said property belonged to the said Wash
and was taken without his consent," is
sufficient. Com . v . Powell , 8 Bush 7 ;
and see Ellis v . Com .,78 Ky . 130.
703

§ 122 ] [Title VIIndictment - Form and Requisites.
(79a ) Unlawful assembly . In an in-
dictment for an unlawful assembly it is
necessaryto allegethat the partiesas-
sembled with intent to aid each other
and to charge the thing intended to be
done . Downes v. Com ., 9 Ky . Opinions
112 .
(80) Unlawfully detaining a woman .
See Ky . Stat., sec. 1158 ; and Wilder v .
Com ., 81 Ky . 591, 5 R. 635; Gargillv.
Com ., 12 R. 149, 13 S. W. 916; Owens
v. Com ., 5 R. 174; Higgins v. Com ., 94
Ky .54, 14 R. 729,21 S. W. 231.
(81) Unnecessary particularity. If a
person or thing necessary to be men-
tioned in an indictment is described with
circumstances of greater particularity
than is requisite, then those circum-
stancesmust be proved. Clark v. Com.,
16 В. М. 207; but an unnecessaryand
immaterial averment will be regarded as
surplusageand it is not necessary to
prove it. Com .v.Jarboe, 89 Ky . 143, 11
R. 344 , 12 S. W. 138 .
(81a) An indictment under Ky . Stat .,
sec. 1241a, contained averments not nec-
essary to describe or constitute the of-
fense; the Commonwealth was not bound
to prove them. Com . v. Hightower, 149
Ky . 563, 149 S. W. 971.
(82 ) Usurpation . For requisitesof an
indictment for usurping the office of
judge of an election, see Wayman v.
Com ., 14 Bush 466. Usurpation of an
office, see Ky . Stat., sec. 1364 ; Wayman
v. Com ., 14 Bush 446 ; Com . v . Adams , 3
Met .6 ; Com .v. Jones , 10 Bush 725.
(82a) Uttering forged check . An in-
dictment for uttering a forged check is
insufficientif it failsto state under
what authority the bank is doing busi-ness. Mason (Alias Scott) v. Com ., 156
Ky . 493, 161 S. W. 229.
(83) Warehouse . For requisitesof in-
dictment under the act in relation to
warehousemen , approved March 6, 1869,
Ky . Stat..sec. 4768 ; see Com . v. Mason ,
82 Ky . 256, 6 R. 244.
(84 ) Weapon . Under an indictment
charging that defendant carried " con-
cealed deadly weapons , to-wit, a bowie
knife, and also a dirk or dagger ," proof
that he carried either will authorize a
conviction. Com . v . Howard , 3 Met .407 ;
Ky .Stat., sec.1309.
(85) Wilful killing. Under sec. 1151,
Ky . Stat., see Conner v . Com ., 13 Bush
714 .
(86) Wounding . Indictmentunder Ky .
Stat ., sec. 1166, need not charge that act
was done feloniously.. Cundiffv. Com .,
26 Ky . 196, 9 R. 537, 5 S. W. 486 ; and
Burns v.Com ., 3 Met . 13 ; Flint v . Com .,
81 Ky . 186, 5 R. 51 ; Gratz v . Com ., 96
Ky . 162, 16 R. 465, 28 S. W. 159 ; as to
wounding in sudden heat and passion,
as described in sec. 1242 , Ky . Stat ., see
Com . v. Hawkins , 11 Bush 603 ; Sosh v .
Com ., 4 R. 254 ; Com . v . Duncan , 91 Ky .
592 , 13 R. 162,16 S. W. 530.
(86a) An indictment which charged
two persons with having maliciously
wounded a person with the intent to kill
him is not bad as chargingmore than
one offense; it charges but one offense.
Com . v . Lawson ,165 Ky .4. 176 S. W. 359.
(87) Filing away - effectof is to post-
pone indefinitelyand court has no au-
thority to so order without consent of
Commonwealth's attorney. Com .v .Cun-
diff, 149 Ky . 37 .
(88) Manslaughter- requisitesof good
indictment. Com . v. Mosser, 133 Ky .
609 , 118 S. W. 915 .
(88a ) It was error to overrule a mo-
tion of the Commonwealth to dismiss a
certain indictment for manslaughter
with leave to resubmit . Com . v . Me-
Candless , 156 Ky .793, 162 S. W. 79.
(89) Billof particulars- when may be
required. Com . v..C. & O. R. Co., 128
Ky .749, 110 S.W. 253.
(90 ) Election offenses. See Com . v.
Drewry , 126 Ky . 183, 103 S. W. 266, 31
R. 632 ; Com . v . Kaufman , 126 Ky . 536,
104 S. W. 740, 31 R. 1064 ; Com . v . Gou-
lett, 137 Ky . 464 , 125 S. W. 1083.
(90a ) Betting on election. An indict-
ment charging that the defendant bet
the sum of $5.00 on the election for
county court clerk of Henderson county
in 1874 did not aver that the bet related
to the electionor defeatof any person
voted for at the election . The indict-
ment was insufficient. Com . v. Cross, 9
Ky . Opinions 109.
(91) Agent of corporation transacting
business without license- requisiteso
indictment. Com . v. Loving, 92 S. W.
575 , 29 R. 175.
(92) Counts in indictment.. See for
fulldiscussionof rightto charge offense
in differentcounts. Com . v.Hargis, 124
Ky . 356, 99 S. W. 348, 30 R. 510 ; Ander-
son v .Com ., 144 Ky .215 , 137 S. W. 1063.
(93 ) Writing that is foundation of in-
dictment should be set out in full or its
absence accounted for. Kinnard v. Com .,
134 Ky . 575, 121 S. W. 489.
(94 ) Sufficiency of indictment gener-
ally in its description of the offense
charged- only necessarythat it should
advise accused of nature of crime . Over-
streetv. Com ., 147 Ky . 471, 144 S. W.
751 ; Com . v . Drewry , 126 Ку . 183, 103
S. W. 266,31 R. 632 .

The indictmentmust be directand certainas regards-
(95 ) An indictment under Ky . Stat.,
sec. 1155, which used the words "wil-
fullyand feloniously" and did not use
the word "unlawfully" was good on de-
murrer . Clark v . Com ., 165 Ky . 472, 177
S. W. 251 .
§ 123. (1) Form . It is not necessary
now that an indictment founded on a
statute should conclude "against the
form of the statute." Com .v.Kennedy ,
15 Β . Μ . 531; nor is it necessary when
the indictment charges time offense was
committed to add "in the year of our
Lord ," or the lettersA. D. Com .v.Tray-
lor,20 R. 97, 45 S. W. 356, 450 .
(2) It isnot necessarythat the style
of the court, or the case, or the formal
part of the indictmentshallbe repeated
in each count when there is more than
one count . Greenwood v . Com ., 11 R.
220, 11 S. W. 811 ; and see Mitchell v .
Com .,106 Ky .602,21 R. 222,51 S. W. 17.
(3) Caption of indictment, omitting
name of county , does not render indict-
ment bad . Johnson v. Com ., 12 R. 835 ,
15 S. W. 662 .
(4 ) Failure of Commonwealth's attor-
ney to sign indictment does not render
it defective. Brown v. Com ., 135 Ky .
635, 117 S. W. 281.
(5 ) See notes to sec. 122 .
§ 124. (1) Certainty in stating of-
fense. A conviction for stealing Philip
Daniel'shog can not be sustainedunder
an indictmentfor stealingStephen Dan-
iel'shog . Hensley v . Com ., 1 Bush 11.
An indictment for murder described the
person murdered as "Barbara Kriel."
The proof disclosedher name to be "Mar-
garet Kriel." The variance was held not
material. Kriel v. Com .. 5 Bush 362 ;
see secs . 125-128 .
(2 ) On the trial of an indictment for
stealing the horse of A , evidence that the
horse stolen belonged to B does not war-
rant a conviction, unless the offense be
sufficientlydescribedin otherrespectsto
identify the property and the act. Mc-
Bride v. Com ., 13 Bush 337 ; and see Car-
ter v . Com ., 25 R. 688, 76 S. W. 337 ;
Riffe v . Com ., 21 R. 1331, 56 S. W. 265 ;
Williams v. Com ., 78 Ky . 93; and sec.
128 .
(3) In a trialfor bigamy it appeared
that firstwife was sometimes calledMag
Sharp (her real name ) and sometimes
Mag White ,while the indictment charged
her name as Mag White ; held not a
fatal variance. Robinson v . Com ., 88
Ky . 386, 10 R. 972, 11 S. W. 210.
(4 ) It is essentialthat the indictment
shall state correctlythe county in which
the offenseis committed . When a change
of venue is obtained and a new indict-
ment found in the county to which the
venue is taken, it shouldstatecorrectly
the county in which offense was com-
mitted . Parker v . Com ., 12 Bush 191.
An indictment charging that offensewas
committed "in the county and circuit
aforesaid" was held sufficient, although
county was not named . Armstrong v .
Com ., 16 R. 494, 29 S. W. 342 ; but the
indictment must charge what offense

that Burlington is in Boone county . It

geon, 9 Ky . Opinions142.
(5) The omissionoftheword "weapon "in an indictment charging defendant
with the offense of "carrying concealed
a deadly " does not vitiate indict-
ment , as the language unmistakably in-
dicates the omitted word , and enables
the courtto supply itwith absolutecer-
tainty. Com . v. Bary , 1 R. 281.
(5a ) A certain indictment was held in-
sufficient,becausealthoughitstatedthe

806 .
(6 ) " It is essentialthat an indictment
contain a statement of the acts consti-
tuting the offense in ordinary and con-
cise language , and in such a manner as
to enable a person of common under-
standingto know what isintended." An
indictmentchargingmurder in the fol-
lowing words is insufficient: "The said
William White heretofore,to wit , on the
day of April, 1871, in the Common-
wealth and county aforesaid did unlaw-
fully, wilfully, maliciously, feloniously
and of his malice aforethoughtkilland
murder his wife, one Nancy White,
against the peace and dignity of the

Bush 714; Com . v. Riley, 14 Bush 44;
Furnish v. Com ., 14 Bush 180 ; Tully v .
Com .,11Bush 154;Alfordv.Com .,84 Ky .
623, 8 R. 550, 2 S. W. 234.
(6a) In an indictment for the embez-
zlementof personalproperty,the proper-
ty must be describedwith the same par-
ticularityas in an indictmentfor lar-
ceny . The descriptionmust be such as to
give the accused notice of the circum-
stances of the exact accusation which he
is calledupon to answer . Clary v .Com .,
163 Ky .48 , 173 S. W. 171.
(6b) If an indictment , for receiving
stolen goods , fails to describe the goods
sufficiently, the indictment will be bad
on demurrer; but thisis no ground for
arrestof judgment. Duncan v.Com .,165
Ky . 247, 176 S. W. 984.
(6c) An indictment for false entry in
bank books was held sufficient. Head v .
Com ., 165 Ky . 339, 176 S. W. 1162.
(6d) A certainindictmentfor operat-
inga poolroom was heldtobe sufficient.
Com . v. Starr, 160 Ky . 260, 169 S. W.
743 .
(7 ) Under an indictment charging that
the defendant carried " concealed deadly
weapons , to-wit , a bowie knife and also
a dirk or dagger," he couldbe convicted
on proof that he carried either. Com . v .
Howard , 3 Met . 407.
(8) Indictment charging A with the
offense" of cutting John Reffitwith in-
tent to kill him ," committed in manner
as follows: "The saidA did unlawfully,
wilfully and feloniously cut, thrust and
stab John Reffit with a knife , from
which cuttingand stabbing said Reffit

814; and seeGratzv.Com ., 96 Ky . 162,

man , 17 R. 217, 30 S. W. 661.

nous liquor" an indictment charging

spirituousliquors" was held insufficient
in not being specificenough . Com . v.
White ,18 B. M. 492 ; but see notes 57-58,

§ 126 [125] [Indictmentto chargebut one offense exception.] An

but one offense,but,ifitmay have been committed indifferentmodes and
by differentmeans ,the indictmentmay allegethe modes and means in the
alternative. (Jurisdictionof alldegrees of offenseincluded, sec. 14 ; de-
grees of offensedefined,sec.263.)

§ 126. (1) Misjoinder of offenses-
what is not . An indictment for murder
contained three paragraphs , "the first
of which charges that the murder was
committed by all five of the accused
jointly; the secondchargesthat Hunter
committed the murder and that the other
defendantsdid with maliceaforethought
counsel, advise and persuade the said
Hunter to killand murder the deceased ;
and in the third paragraph or count it
is charged that the appellant (Thomp-
son) committed the murder ,and that the
other defendants in like manner coun-
seled and advised the commission of the
offense ." Held that there was no mis-
joinder of offenses, that but one offense
was charged, although allegedto have
been committed in differentmodes.
Thompson v. Com ., 1 Met .13 ; and see to
same effect,Angel v . Com ., 14 R. 10, 18
S. W. 849 ; Benge v . Com ., 92 Ky . 1, 13
K. 308, 17 S. W. 146; Cupp v. Com ., 87
Ky . 35, 9 R. 877, 7 S. W. 405 ; Howard
v. Com ., 110 Ky . 356, 22 R. 1845, 61 S.
W. 756 .
(la) An indictment which charges the
defendant with shooting and wounding
two personsnamed with intent to kill,
means that he wounded both of them
with a single shot. Rutherford v . Com .,
9 Ky .Opinions 163.
(2) An indictment charging that de-
fendant did "unlawfully set-up, exhibit
and keep for himself and another a faro
bank ," charges but one offense. Vowels
v .Com ., 84 Ky . 52, 8 R. 74.
(3) An indictment charging the taking
at the same time and by the same act of
one silver dollar and a warrant of arrest
issued against the accused and properly
in the hands of the person robbed for
executionas an officer, does not allege
two offenses. Lislev. Com ., 82 Ky .250,
6 R. 229 .
(4 ) An indictment for "breaking a
storehouse with intent to steal and steal-
ing articlestherefrom" chargesbut one
offense,althoughitallegesboth a break-
ing with intentto stealand an actual
stealing. Farrisv.Com ., 90 Ky . 637, 12
R. 592, 14 S. W. 681.
(5) If severalarticles, each belonging
to differentpersons, be stolenat same
time , only one offense has been com-
mitted . Nichols v. Com .,78 Ky . 180; and
so if the severalarticlesare owned by
the same person, 1 Bush 211 .
(5a) An indictment for hog stealing
which failsto charge that they were
stolenat the same time or that they
were jointpropertyof P and others,was
held to be insufficient on demurrer .
Stephens v.Com ., 164 Ky .265,175 S. W.
353 .
(5b) An indictment, which charged
two persons with having maliciously
wounded a person with the intent to kill
bim , is not bad as charging more than
one offense; it charges but one offense.
Com . v. Lawson , 165 Ky . 4, 176 S. W.
359 .
(5c) An indictment which charges a
person with feloniouslybreaking intoa
storehouseand feloniouslytaking, steal-
ing and carrying away therefrom articles
of value isnot bad forduplicity. Drury
v . Com ., 162 Ky . 123, 172 S. W. 94.

fendant destroyed, injuredand carried

committed in differentmodes and by dif-
ferent means , the indictment may allege
the modes and means in the alternative .
Com . v. Lowe , 116 Ky . 335, 25 R. 534, 76
S. W. 119 .
(7a) An indictment may charge the
commission of a crime in different modes
and in several counts ; under such an in-
dictment the accused may be convicted

Ellis v. Com ., 78 Ky . 130 ; and see Com .

cessory. See Com . v . Hargis , 124 Ky .
356, 99 S. W. 348 ,30 R. 510, and notes to

(11) When election proper or neces-
sary . If,upon the trialof an indictment
charging but one offense, evidence is in-
troduced for the Commonwealth of dif-
ferentacts,any one of which might con-
stitutethe offense, it is proper for the
prosecutor to elect upon which of them
he will rely. If he makes no such elec-
tion the court may elect. McCreary v.

§ 127. (1) Misjoinder- practice. See
indictmentchargingtwo offensesunder
secs. 1256, 1257,Ky . Stat .; Ellisv . Com .,
78 Ky . 130; and see further, Com . v .
Powell , 8 Bush 7 ; Com . v. Searls, 3 R.
394; and see as to practice where there
is a misjoinder, secs. 165, 168.
(2) Offenses that may be joined.
Grand larcenyand receivingstolengoods
may be joined. Upton v. Com ., 14 R.
165; but the offenseof forgingand utter-
ing a forged paper can not be joined.
Huff v. Com ., 19 R. 1064, 42 S. W. 907.
(3 ) Embezzlement - and fraudulent
entriesin publicbooks can not be joined.
Com . v . Bradley , 132 Ky . 512, 116 S. W.
761 .
(4) Demurrer is proper mode of ob-
jecting to indictment for misjoinder..
Com . v.Bradley,132 Ky . 512, 116 S. W.
761; Johnson v. Com .,90 Ky . 488, 12 R.
442,14 S.W. 492.

Ifan offenseinvolvethecommissionof,oran attempttocommit an injury

§ 129 [130] [Time offensecommitted- statementas to.] The state-
ment in the indictment,as to the time at which the offensewas committed,
is not material further than as a statement that it was committed before
thetimeof findingtheindictment,unlessthetimebe a materialingredient

it must prove fatal to the prosecution,
unless the offense be sufficientlyde-

(2 ) In an indictment for housebreak-
ing it is not necessary to describe the
owner of the house broken or the prop-
erty taken with any more precision or
certainty than is necessary to inform the
accused of the nature of the accusation
against him and to bar a second convic-
tion. Johnson v . Com ., 87 Ky . 189, 10

(3) In an indictment for burglary a
description of the house entered will be
sufficientthat will bar another prosecu-
tion for the same offense. Olive v.Com .,

(4 ) Under an indictment for obtain-
ing money under falsepretenses it is not

longed to the persons alleged in the in-
dictment . Hennessy v. Com ., 88 Ky . 301,
10 R ..823 , 11 S. W. 13 .
(4a) An indictment for obtaining
money by false pretenses did not allege
that the person from whom it was ob-
tained, owned the money . The indict-
ment was sufficient. Hayes v . Com ., 173
Ky . 188,190 S. W. 700.
(5) An indictment charging the ac-
cused with stealingproperty from the
"Columbus Stave Co." need not have
allegedthat it was incorporated, but
having so alleged it was not necessary
to prove it. Com . v .Vineyard , 26 R. 543,
82 S. W. 289 ; and see Jackson v . Com .,
17 R. 1197, 34 S. W. 14.
(5a) A misdescriptionof the owner-
shipof propertyin an indictmentforre-
ceiving stolen goods , knowing same to
have been stolen , was immaterial . Com .
v. McGarvey , 158 Ky . 570, 165 S. W. 973.
(6) Misdescriptionof ownership or
use of property in arson is not fatal to
indictment. Com . v. Napier , 84 S. W.
536, 27 R. 131 ; Overstreet v . Com ., 147
Ky . 471, 144 S. W. 751.
(7) Robbery - misdescription of name
of person not material if property is
well described. Bibb v . Com ., 112 S. W.
401 .
(8) False personation--error in name
of falsepersonatorwhen not material.
Com . v. Vaughn , 140 Ky . 559, 131 S. W.
396 .
(9 ) Assault when error in name of
person assaulted not material. Robin-
son v. Com ., 144 Ку . 111, 137 S. W. 833.
(10) Subornation of perjury- When
error in name of person suborned not
material. Henderson v. Com ., 122 Ky .
296 , 28 R. 1212 , 91 S. W. 1141 .
§ 129. (1 ) Time - Statement as to.
An indictment found at the May term ,
1865, which charges that the offensewas
committed on the day of July , 1865,
and afterward in describing the offense
in the indictment it is in express terms
charged to have been committed before
the finding of the indictment , is good .
Jones v.Com ., 1 Bush 34 .
(2) In a prosecution for nuisance the
Commonwealth was not confined to the
710

Title VI ] [§ 129Indictment- Form and Requisites.
day alleged in the indictment, but had
the rightto prove the commission of the
offenseon any day within twelve months
before the finding of the indictment . C.
R. Co. v. Com ., 80 Ky . 137, 3 R. 639;
Com . v. Ky . Highland R. Co., 147 Ky .
409 , 145 S. W. 386.
(2a ) In a trialof an indictment under
Ky . Stat., sec. 1155, the Commonwealth
may prove the commission of the offense
at any time prior to the finding of the
indictment. McCreary v. Com ., 158 Ky .
612 , 165 S. W. 981 .
(3) The court is authorized on de-
murrer to assume that the offense was
committedat the time chargedin the
indictment, although the Commonwealth
on the trial would not be confined to the
time alleged. Com . v. Cain, 14 Bush 525 .
(4 ) An indictment which charges that
the offense was committed on the same
day the indictment is filedin court will
be good if the indictment charged that
the offense was committed before the
time of finding the indictment . Com . v .
Miller, 79 Ky . 451, 3 R. 231.
(4a ) An indictment returned May 11,
1916 , charged that the offense was com-
mitted May 11, 1916, and did not ex-
pressly state that it was committed be-
fore the finding of the indictment . The
indictment was sufficient. Morgan v .
Com .,172 Ky .684,189 S. W. 943.
(5 ) Averiment that offense was com-
mitted on the day of July , 1883, im-
ports the commission of it anterior to
the 24th of the saine month , when in-
dictment was filed. Vowels v. Com ., 84
Ky . 52. 8 R. 74 .
(6) In a prosecution for false swear-
ing the date of the commission of the
offense is not material, and a variance
between date charged and record of pro-
ceeding in which false oath was taken
does not render record incompetent .
Com . v . Davis , 94 Ky . 612, 15 R. 262, 23
S. W. 218; Richey v. Com ., 81 Ky . 524,
5 R. 524 .
(7 ) Where the defendant is indicted
for different offenses of the same char-
acter committed within a year , the Com-
monwealth may in one trialconfineitself
to one act, and it will not be a bar to
other trials. C. & O. R. Co. v . Com ., 88
Ky . 368, 10 R. 919, 11 S. W. 87 ; see note
29 to sec . 176 .
(8) An indictment for bigamy charged
that on the day of September , 1891,
the accused married , having then a wife ,
to whom he was married on September
19. 1891 ; the court held that the date
was not material , and that figures 1891
were used by mistake in place of 1881.
Faustre v . Com ., 92 Ky . 34, 13 R. 347,
17 S. W. 189; and see Williams v . Com .,
18 R. 667, 37 S. W. 839.
(9 ) An indictment for housebreaking ,
found on December 28, 1891, alleged that
offense was committed on December 29,
1891, and contained no specificaverment
that offensewas committed prior to find-
ing of indictment; held good. Williams
v.Com ., 13 R. 893, 18 S. W. 1024 .
(10) An indictmentfor perjurystated
that offense was committed October 1,
1892; the evidence showed that trial
when accused swore falsely occurred
September 19, 1892 ; the variance was
held immaterial . Com . v. Davis , 94 Ky .
612,15 R. 262,23 S. W. 218 ; and see Com .
v .Miles , 140 Ky . 577, 131 S. W. 385.
(11) Two indictments for same offense
committed at different times . See as to
when proof in one case is and is not a
bar to prosecution of other indictment.
C. & O. R. R. v. Com ., 88 Ky . 368, 10 R.
919 , 11 S. W. 87 ; Cawein v . Com ., 110
Ky . 273,22 R. 1734, 61 S. W. 275.
(12) An indictment for murder failed
to state when crime was committed ,but ,
as there was no demurrer filed to it, the
court would not consider the objection
made . Lightfootv. Com ., 80 Ky . 516, 4
R. 463 .
(12a) Time is not a material ingred-
ient in the offense of murder . May v .
Com ., 153 Ky . 141, 154 S. W. 1074.
(13) In misdemeanor cases time is ma-
terial, and if indictment shows that of-
fense is barred by limitation a demurrer
should be sustained. Williams v . Com .,
18 R. 667 , 37 S. W. 839 ; Combs v . Com .,
27 R. 273 , 84 S. W. 753 ; and see Kehoe
v . Com ., 28 R. 35, 88 S. W. 1107.
(13a) A certain indictment under Ky .
Stat ., sec. 1138, was found on September
16, 1913,and alleged the sale of liquor to
have occurred July 4, 1912, within twelve
months just prior to the finding of the
indictment . The indictment was suffi-
cient. Paul v . Com ., 159 Ky . 848, 169 S.
W. 544 .
(14) Indictment in misdemeanor cases
may charge offense on any day within
a year and conviction may be had on
proof of guilt within the year . Com . v .
Miles , 140 Ky . 577, 131 S. W. 385.
(15) Time may be specifiedin indict-
ment and evidence and thus avoid one
conviction barring another. Shirley v .
Com ., 143 Ky . 183, 132 S. W. 227.
(16) A conviction on a plea of guilty
is not a bar to a prosecution for an of-
fense committed on a day other than
the day specifiedin the indictment . 175
Ky . 155, 192 S. W. 1029.

stitutingjurisdiction; but thejudgment or determinationmay be stated
as given or made ,or the proceedingshad . The factsconstitutingjurisdic-
tionmust ,however ,be establishedon the trial.

forlibelneednotsetforthany extrinsicfacts,forthepurposeof showing

sufficientto stategenerallythatthesame was publishedconcerninghim.

otheroffense,have been withheldor destroyedby theactor procurement

dictment, and proved on the trial, a misdescriptionof the instrument is
immaterial .

§ 136. (1) Statute- indictment under .
"Where the words of the statute are de-
scriptive of the offense, the indictment
will be sufficient if it shall follow the
language and expressly charge the de-
scribed offense of the defendant .
this rule applies only to offenses which
are complete in themselves, when the
acts set out in the statute have been
done or performed ." Davis v . Com ., 13
Bush 318; Ward v. Com ., 14 Bush 233 ;
Mitchell v . Com ., 88 Ky . 349, 10 R. 910,
11 S. W. 209; Com . v.Grinstead,108 Ky .
59 , 21 R. 1444, 55 S. W. 720 ; Com . v .
Landis , 129 Ky . 445,112 S. W. 581.
(2) An indictment under the "Kuklux
law" will be sufficientif it charges the
offense in the words of the statute . Sell-
ers v. Com ., 13 Bush 331 ; Buchanan v .
Com ., 95 Ky . 334, 15 R. 738, 25 S. W.
265 ; Ky . Statutes, sec. 1223.
(3) "Where the words of the statute
are descriptiveof the offense, the indict-
ment should follow the language and
expresslycharge the describedoffense
against the defendant , or it will be de-
fective." Com . v . Tanner , 5 Bush 316 ;
Com . v . Turner , 8 Bush 1; Conner v .
Com . , 13 Bush 714.
(4 ) "When a statute contains provisos
or exceptions in distinct clauses, it is
not necessary to state in the indictment
that the defendant does not come within
the exceptions or to negative the pro-
visos it contains." Com .v . Bull, 13 Bush
656 ; Com . v . Bierman , 13 Bush 345 ; but
" if the statute creating an offense con-
tains in its enacting clause exceptions, it
is necessary to negative such exceptions
in the indictment so as to show that the
defendant does not come within any of
them ." Conner v . Com ., 13 Bush 714 ;
Knoxville Nursery Co. v. Com ., 108 Ky .
6 , 21 R. 1483, 55 S. W. 691 ; Com . v . L.
& N. R. Co., 140 Ky . 21, 130 S. W. 798 .
(5) Under the statute providing that
it should be an offenseto carry concealed
deadly weapons except by certainper-
sons, who were specified, it was not nec-
essary to state that the defendant did
not come within any of the exceptions.
Com .v.McClanahan ,2 Met .8 .
(6 ) " If a statute refers to a common
law offenseby itspopular name , as,for

pose a penaltyfor itscommission, it is

ously" unless the statute requires that

(11) An indictmentchargingthat"the
defendantdid unlawfully, feloniously
and maliciously, with intent to kill him ,
cut and wound one Philip Yeiser," is suf-
ficientunder sec. 2, art. 6, chap. 29,
Gen. Stat . [now sec. 1166, Ky . Stat .].
Flint v. Com ., 81 Ky . 186,5 R. 51 ; Gratz
v . Com ., 96 Ky . 162, 16 R. 465, 28 S. W.
159 .
(12) Aider and abettor in statutory
crime may be punished unless it clearly
appear that its provisions were only in-
tended to apply to party actually com-
mitting crime . Com . v. Carter, 94 Ky .
527, 15 R. 253, 23 S. W. 344 ; overruling
Stamper v.Com .,7 Bush 612.
(13) Although an offense is not
charged in the precise words used in the
statute in defining it, if it is alleged in
words conveying the same meaning it
will be sufficient. Taylor v . Com ., 3
Bush 508 ; Moore v. Com ., 92 Ky . 630, 13
R. 738, 18 S. W. 853 ; Gratz v . Com ., 96
Ky . 162,16 R. 465,28 S. W. 159.

§ 159 [160] [Proceedingswhen indictmentsetaside.] Ifthemotion
be sustained,the court shallmake an order thatthe case be submittedto
anothergrandjury,tobe assembledatthator thenextterm of thecourt,
and thedefendant,ifincustody,shallbe remanded to jail,or ifon bail,
the bail shallbe liablefor the defendant'sappearance to answer a new
indictment,ifone be found. (See further,as toreferencetograndjury,
scs.116,117 ; dismissalwhen not a bar,sec.178.)
Cross reference: See Gregory's Ky . Crim . Law , secs. 963, 1139.
(2) A white person indictedby a
grand jury composed wholly of persons
of the white race can not complain be-
cause negroes were excludedby statute
from the jury. Com . v. Wright,79 Ky .
22 , 1 R. 258. The statute declaring that
only white persons were competent as
jurorswas declaredunconstitutionalin
Com . v. Johnson , 78 Ky . 509, 1 R. 108.(3) Jury commissioners or sheriff,
when he acts in their stead, are not
bound to selectnegro jurors; the only
right the accused can claim is that no
citizenotherwise competent shall be cx-
cluded by law on account of race or
color. Haggard v.Com .,79 Ky .366,2 R.
356 .
(4 ) The failureof any member of the
grand jury to possesseach of the quali-
ficationsprescribedby the statute, viz.:
being a housekeeper, citizen, and over
twenty -one years of age (Ky . Stat ., sec.
2248 ), is not cause for setting aside in-
dictments found by such jury. It was
formerly held to be cause . 14 Bush 457 ;
10 Bush 476 ; but this rule has been
changed by Ky . Stat., sec. 2248.
(5) "It isa well-settledprinciple, and
one which is applicableto the actionof
all juries, that the testimony of the
jurors is not competent to explain the
grounds of theirdecisionor impeach thevalidity of their finding." Com . V.
Skeggs , 3 Bush 19 .
(6) That a member of a grand jury
was a school trustee when the indict-
ment was found isno ground forsetting
it aside. The provision of the statute
(Ky . Stat.,sec.2248) that no "civiloffi-
cer" shall be competent does not make
the taking of a civilofficeron the grand
jury a substantialerrorin itsformation.
Com . v.Pritchett, 11 Bush 277; Com .v.
Rudd , 3 R. 328; nor is it ground for
quashing an indictmentthat one of the
grand jurors that found it was the owner
of a gristmill. Slagelv. Com ., 81 Ky .
485 , 5 R. 545 .
(7) It did not appear in the order of
courtthat the grand jury was sworn, or
that one of their number was appointed
foreman ; but , in the absence of proof to
the contrary, thecourtwillpresume that
these requirementswere complied with.

§ 168. (1) Misjoinder practice. If
there is a misjoinder and attorney does
not dismiss one of the offenses,demurrer
should be sustained. Ellis v. Com ., 78
Ky . 130 ; and see Nichols v . Com ., 78 Ky .
180 ; Johnson v. Com ., 90 Ky . 488, 12 R.

(2) Upon demurrer for misjoinderof
offenses it was error to refuse to allow

charge he would prosecute. Com . v.
Goins , 9 Ky . Opinions 108.
(3) If upon a trial of an indictment
charging one offense, evidence is intro-
duced of differentacts, any one of which
might constitute the offense, the election
of the Commonwealth to relyupon one
of these acts to support a convictiondoes
not amount to nolle prosequi as to the
other acts. McCreary v . Com ., 163 Ky .
206 , 173 S. W. 251 .
§ 169. (1) Demurrer - bar . An errone-
ous judgment for the defendant sustain-
ing a demurrer to an indictment, upon
the ground that it contains a misjoinder
of offenses, does not operate as a bar to
a future prosecution, and may be re-
versed by the Court of Appeals. Com .
v.Anthony ,2 Met . 399; itisthe indict-
ment and not the opinion of the lower
court that must determine whether the
judgment on demurrer shalloperate as
a bar . Com . v . Cain , 14 Bush 525, and
see notes to sec . 178 .
(2 ) Demurrer to indictment if sus-
tained is not a bar to a subsequent in-
dictment for same offense unless the first
indictment contained matter that was a
bar or legal defense. Com . v . Cook , 102
Ky . 288 , 19 R. 1336, 43 S. W. 400 ; Code ,
sec . 165 .

§ 172. (1) Failure to plead. Does the
silenceor failureof the accused to plead
amount to a confession of guilt? The
court says: "The saferrule would be,
where the accused refused to file any
plea, to have the plea of not guiltyen-
teredand counselassigned; but when he
does plead, no confession,exceptthat of
confession of guilt in open court by the
accused inperson,willauthorizea judg-
ment of conviction." Triplett v . Com .,
84 Ky . 193, 8 R. 67, 1 S. W. 84.
(2) Pleas that may be entered- evi-
dence . There are but three kinds of
pleasthat can be entered: First,guilty;
second, not guilty; and, third, former
convictionor acquittal; and the court
may refuseto allowany otherpleato be
entered. Although theremay be a plea
of guilty, the Commonwealth to increase,
or the defendant to mitigate, the punish-
ment , has the right to place the facts
beforethe jury. Cornelisonv.Com .,84
Ky . 583, 8 R. 793, 2 S. W. 235 ; and re-
quirement that indictmentshallbe read
as providedin sec.219,and plea entered
is mandatory . Galloway v . Com ., 5 R.
213 .
(3) Plea not entered. Although the
orders fail to show that a plea of not
guilty was entered, when the instruc-
tions show what the issue was , it is too
late to raise the question in the Court
of Appeals that no plea was entered or
issue made . Meece v. Com ., 78 Ky . 586,
1 R. 337 .
(4 ) See notes to sec. 176.
(5 ) Traverse not necessary. A plea of
former acquittal or conviction need not
be traversed by the Commonwealth . The
burden is on the accused to show that
he has been acquitted or convicted of the
identical offense for which he is being
tried. Vowels v . Com ., 83 Ky . 193, 7 R.
176 .
(6) Trial in absence of accused. On
an indictment for misdemeanor the de-
fendant may be triedin his absence , and
may by counselput in any plea save
that of guilty. Johnson v . Com ., 1 Duv .
244; Com . v . Neat , 89 Ку . 241 , 11 R.
434, 12 S. W. 256.

§ 173 [173] [Plea of guiltymust be by defendantin person.] The

quittal,may be giveninevidenceunderit.

stance in the indictmenton which the acquittalor convictiontook place.

§ 173. See notes to sec . 174 .
§ 174. (1) Plea of guilty may be
withdrawn even afterverdict,a new trial
being granted for that purpose , if it ap-
pear that defendant was induced to enter
the plea by the threatsor promises of
the court or attorney for the Common-
wealth, and thereby overreachedor de-
ceived, Mounts v . Com ., 89 Ky . 274, 11
R. 474 ,12 S. W. 311 ; and it may be with-
drawn and plea of not guilty entered
after evidence has been introduced . Wil-
liamsv. Com ., 25 R. 2041, 80 S. W. 173.
(2) Plea of guilty- evidence. A plea
of guilty does not deprive the Common-
wealth of the right to introduce evidence
bearing upon the question of punish-
ment where the offensecharged is of dif-
ferentdegrees, or is punishablein the
discretionof the jury, unless such plea
has been entered upon the faith of prom-
isesby the courtor Commonwealthat-torney that the minimum punishment
willbe inflicted. Mounts v.Com .,89 Ky .
275, 11 R. 474, 12 S. W. 311; Cornelison
v . Com ., 84 Ky . 583, 8 R. 793, 2 S. W.
235; Williams v . Com .,25 R. 2041, 80 S.
W. 173 .
(3) Plea of guilty entered through fear
of violence- new trialshould be granted .
Littlev. Com ., 142 Ky . 92, 133 S. W.
1149 .
(4) Plea of guilty by advice of coun-
selwill not be set aside. White v. Com .,
140 Ky . 9, 130 S. W. 796 .
(5) Plea of guilty in justice'scourt
does not prevent appeal to circuitcourt
and on appeal case will be triedde novo .
Holtman v .Com ., 129 Ky . 710, 112 S. W.
851 .
§ 175. (1) Plea of not guilty. "The
plea of not guiltyputs in issue every
factnecessaryforthe Commonwealth to
establishin order to a conviction, and as
to all these facts or issues, the burden
of establishingthem by proof that ex-
cludes a reasonable doubt remains with
the Commonwealth ; but when the ac-
cused reliesupon some separate matter
of defense not embraced in those issues,
the burden of proof to establish such
matter is on the accused." Ball v.Com .,
81 Ky .662,5 R. 787 .
(2 ) A plea of "not guilty" does not
put in issue surplusage in an indictment .
Siegelv. Com ., 177 Ky . 232, 197 S. W.
809 .
§ 176. (1) Application of rule. The
law as to former jeopardyextendsto all
feloniesand to allcaseswhere the pun-
ishment inflictedis infamous . Williams
v. Com .,78 Ky .93 ; Robinson v . Com ., 88
Ky . 386, 10 R. 972, 11 S. W. 210 ; and see
Cooper v . Com ., 106 Ky . 909, 21 R. 546 ,
51 S. W. 789, holding that where a per-
son is acquitted he can not be indicted
for false swearing on trial at which he
was acquitted.
(2 ) Arrest of judgment . Where judg-
ment is arrested or reversed because of
729

§ 176] [Title VIArraignment and Pleadings by Defendant .
error in not sustaining motion in arrest
of judgment, defendant may be tried
under a new indictment . Sec. 279 ; Cor-
nelius v . Com ., 3 Met . 481 ; White ,v .
Com ., 9 Bush 178.
(3) Breach of the peace- bars what .
A conviction for a breach of the peace
is a bar to a subsequentindictmentfor
malicious wounding for the same act.
Com .v.Bright,78 Ky .238; but inReddy
v. Com ., 97 Ky .784, 17 R. 536, 31 S. W.
730 ; this case is partiallyoverruled, and
it is held that where the same acts con-
stitute one or more misdemeanors that a
prosecution for one of the misdemeanors
willbar a prosecutionfor any other on
the same state of facts; but a prosecu-
tion in an inferior court for a misde-
meanor will not bar an indictment for a
felony upon the same facts. And a fine
for a breach of the peace is a bar to an
indictment for assault and battery for
the same offense. Com . v .Foster,3 Met .
1; Linch v. Com ., 18 R. 145, 35 S. W.
264 .
(3a) Contempt . Punishment for con-
tempt in assaulting a witness does not
bar a prosecution for the same assault.
Long v.Com .,177 Ky . 391, 197 S. W. 843.
(4 ) Consent of accused to discharge of
jury waives any objectionto being tried
again, and his consent may appear by
implicationas well as by expresswords,
but his silence will not be construed into
consent. Robinson v.Com ., 88 Ky . 386,
10 R. 972 , 11 S. W. 210 .
(5 ) Definition of former jeopardy . "A
person is in legaljeopardy when he is
put upon trialbeforea court of compe-
tentjurisdictionupon indictmentor in-
formation which is sufficient in form and
substance to sustain a conviction and a
jury has been charged with his deliver-
ance ; and a jury is said to be thus
charged when they have been impaneled
and sworn ." Williams v. Com ., 78 Ky .
93 ; Gaskins v . Com ., 97 Ky . 494, 17 R.
352, 30 S. W. 1017 ; Com . v . Goulett, 140
Ky . 843, 132 S. W. 151 ; Drake v . Com .,
150 Ky . 374,96 S. W. 580 ,29 R. 982.
(6) Degrees of offense. Conviction or
acquittal of any degree bar to prosecu-
tion for any other degree. Sec. 177;
Com . v. Bright, 78 Ky . 238; but see
Reddy v. Com ., 97 Ky . 784 , 17 R. 536, 31
S. W. 730 ; partiallyoverruling this case,
and secs. 262-3-4 and notes thereto as to
degrees of offenses.
(7) Discharge of jury after defendant
has been put upon trial, save in case of
necessityor by his consent, operatesas
an acquittal and bars a retrial. Robin-
son v . Com ., 88 Ky . 386, 10 R. 972, 11 S.
W. 210; Williams v.Com ..78 Ky . 93.
(8) When jury may be discharged see
88 Ky . 386, secs.250,251. Secs.243. 252,
in so far they authorize the dismissal of
the indictment and discharge of the jury
in all cases and say it will not be a bar,
are unconstitutional. Williams v. Com .,
78 Ky . 93 .
(9) Dischargeof jury and dismissalof
indictment and resubmission to another
grand jury isa bar to prosecutionunder
second indictment if first indictment
would have sustained a conviction . Col-
lierv. Com ., 90 Ky . 262, 12 R. 160, 13 S.
W. 922; Robinson v. Com ., 88 Ky . 386,
10 R. 972,11 S. W. 210 ; Williams v .Com .,
78 Ky . 93 ; and this rule applies, al-
though second indictment may charge
another offense growing out of same
facts. Huff v .Com ., 19 R. 1064, 42 S. W.
907 ; Triplett v .Com .,84 Ky . 193, 8 R. 67,
1 S. W. 84 ; Com .v .Goulett ,140 Ky . 843,
132 S. W. 151; Drake v. Com ., 96 S. W.
580 , 29 R. 982 .
(10) Discharge of jury because of their
failure to agree does not operate as an
acquittal or as a bar to another trial,
although they are dischargedin absence
of accused. Yarbrough v. Com ., 89 Ky .
151, 11 R. 351, 12 S. W. 143.
(11) After the jury had been sworn
and while the evidence was being heard ,
one of the jurors announced that he was
on the grand jury that found the indiet-
ment . The court, againstthe objection
of the defendant,orderedthe jurorto be
discharged and another selected, which
was done . The defendant was convicted ;
on appeal the case was reversed, and on
the second trial he pleaded in bar the
dischargeof the juror. Held that his
plea should have been sustained,O'Brian
v . Com ., 9 Bush 333, overruling O'Brian
v . Com ., 6 Bush 563.
(lla) Distinct crime- prosecution not
barred . An acquittal or dismissal of an
indictment does not bar a prosecution
under an indictment for another distinct
crime. Head v. Com ., 174 Ky . 841, 192
S. W. 861 .
(12) Election to prosecute for misde-
meanor . Re -referenceto grand jury.be-
fore defendant has been put upon trial,
is not a bar to prosecution under second
indictment for felony, although order
electing to prosecute under firstindict-
ment - which was also for a felony--for
a misdemeanor was not set aside. King
v. Com ., 15 R. 247 , 22 S. W. 750.
(13) Larceny of several articles at
same time . By the same act , and with
the same intent, F took a horse, wagon
730

Title VI ] [§ 176Arraignment and Pleadings by Defendant .
and harness,the propertyof H. Two in-
dictmentswere found againstF ,one for
stealingthe horse,the otherforstealing
the wagon and harness. On the trialfor
stealingthe horse F pleadednot guilty
andwas acquitted. Thisacqacquittalwas
a good pleain bar to the indictmentfor
stealingthe wagon and harness. Fisher
v.Com.,1Bush211; seeNicholsv.Com.,78 Ky. 180; Williamsv. Com ., 78 Ky .93 , and prosecution for larceny is bar to
prosecution forburglary of same articles.
Triplett v. Com ., 84 Ky . 193, 8 R. 67, 1
S. W. 84 ; but where a person presents a
pistol at two persons and compels both
to surrender their property at once hẹ
is guilty of two offenses. Keeton v.
Com ., 92 Ky . 522, 13 R. 748, 18 S. W.
359 ; and so where one in an attempt to
commit burglary shoots and wounds ,
Man v. Com .,25 R. 2281, 79 S. W. 230.
(14) Larceny - misnomer of owner of
property. The defendant was charged in
one count of the indictment with grand
larceny and in another with receiving
stolen goods . He pleaded not guilty, and
after the evidence was heard the indict-
ment , on motion of the Commonwealth's
attorney ,was dismissed. Afterward an-
other indictment was found similar to
the one dismissed,except that it charged
that the stolen property was owned by A
and B , while the first stated that it was
owned by B. To his indictment the de-
fendant's plea of former acquittal should
have been sustained. Williams v . Com .,
78 Ky . 93 ; but an indictment for steal-
ing cattle from "A " that is dismissed,
doesnotbaran indictmentforstealing
cattle from "B." although the cattle
stolenwere the same . Riffev. Com ., 21
R. 1331, 56 S. W. 265 ; and see Carter v .
Com .,25 R. 688, 76 S. W. 337, and notes
to sec . 124 .
(15) Mistrial- new indictment - for-
mer indictment lost or defective - second
prosecution not barred by firstmistrial
and discharge of jury. Thompson v.
Com ., 15 R. 838,25 S. W. 1059.
(16) New trialplacesparty in same
position as ifno trialhad been had . Sec.
270; Com .v.Arnold,83 Ky . 1, 6 R. 181.
(17 ) Accused can not plead firsttrial
in bar - and after new trial has been
granted firstindictment may be dis-
missed and new indictment found . Wells
v.Com .,9 R. 658,6 S. W. 150; Haskins v.
Com ., 8 R. 419, 1 S. W. 730; and see
note 21 .
(18) Omission to arraign defendant-
dischargeof jury. Where the jury isse-
lected and sworn and some evidence in-
troduced and it is then discovered that
defendant has not been arraigned, the
courtmay ,upon refusalof defendantto
act, dischargejury, arraignaccused, re-
swear jury and proceed with trial. Dis-
ney v .Com .,9 R. 413, 5 S. W. 360 ; Minor
v. Com ., 5 R. 176 ; and see further, as to
arraignment,notesto section154.
-
(19) Pardon effect of. A pardon
pendingtheprosecution,but beforecon-viction, isa good pleainbar to the in-
dictment . Com . v . Bush , 2 Duv . 264.
(20 ) One who has been convicted of a
felony and pardoned by the Governor on
a subsequent commission of the same
offense may be convicted and sentenced
for double the time as provided in sec.
12, art. 1, chap . 29, Gen. Stat . [now sec.
1130, Ky . Stat.]. Mount v. Com ., 2
Duv . 83 .
(21) Reversal upon detective indict-
ment . Accused can not plead former
conviction to a trial under a new indict-
ment . Mount v . Com ., 2 Duv . 93 ; White
v . Com ., 9 Bush 178; and where one has
been convictedof manslaughter under a
murder indictment,he may ,upon reversal
of case, be tried again for murder . Com .
v. Arnold, 83 Ky . 1, 6 R. 181; and see
note 17 .
(22) Shooting at random on public
highway is a bar to a prosecutionon
same state of facts for flourishinga pis-
tol in a threatening manner . Carman v .
Com ., 25 R. 1048, 76 S. W. 1078.
(23) Surety of tavern-keeper. The
surety of a tavern -keeper is not con-
cludedby a judgmentagainstthetavern-
keeperfora breachofhisbond,butmaycontesthis liability, and is entitledto
ten days ' notice and to a jury trial. Mar-
goleyv.Com .,3 Met .406.
(24) Trialbeginswhen jury is sworn.
Willis v. Com ., 85 Ky . 68, 8 R. 63, 2 S.
W. 654 ; Tye v. Com ., 3 R. 59 ; Collierv .
Com .,110 Ky .516,22 R. 1929,62 S.W. 4.
(25 ) Trial in inferiorcourt. When a
person has been indicted for a misde-
meanor he may be triedby the quarterly
court under the act of March 18 , 1876
[Ky .Stat.,sec.1073]; but ifhe procures
a trialunder said act, not in good faith,
but merely for the purpose of avoiding
a trial in the circuitcourt, a judgment
of acquittalin the quarterlycourt will
not be a bar to a trial under the indict-
ment in the circuitcourt. Carrington v.
Com ., 78 Ky . 83 ; Reddy v . Com ., 97 Ky .
784, 17 R. 536, 31 S. W. 730.
(26 ) Trial under ordinance of a town
was held,in Kemper v.Com .,85 Ky .219,
S R. 763.3 S.W. 159; not to bar a prose-
cution for same offenseby the Common-

§ 177-178] [Title VIArraignment and Pleadingsby Defendant.
§ 177 [177] [Acquittalor convictionbarsalldegreesof offense.] If
an offenseconsistof differentdegrees,a conviction,or acquittal,by judg-
ment upon a verdict,shallbe a bar toanotherprosecutionfortheoffense
inany ofitsdegrees. (Degreesofoffensedefined,sec.263.)
Cross reference: See Gregory'sKy .Crim.Law ,sec.1007.
§ 178 [178] [Dismissalthatdoesnot operateas bar.] The dismissal
of theindictmentby thecourt,on demurrer,exceptasprovidedinsection
169 [or foran objectionto itsform or substancetakenon the trial,or
forvariancebetween the indictmentand theproof* ],shallnot bar another
prosecutionfor the same offense.
*See notesto thissectionand to sections243 and 252.--Ed .
Cross reference: See Gregory'sKy .Crim.Law ,secs.963,1008.
wealth , but see now Constitution, sec.
168, changing this rule.
(27) Trial in United States court. A
defendant can not be convicted in a
State court for an offense after having
been tried and convicted for the same
offense in the United States court . Com .
v. Overby, 80 Ky . 208, 3 R. 704; but if
United States court had no jurisdiction
of offense trialby it is not bar . Blyew
v. Com .,91 Ky .200,12 R. 742, 15 S. W.
356 .
(27a ) Two offenses committed by
fraudulent deposit of two checks at one
time- "cold check law ." Two checks
drawn by differentdrawers in favor of
the same payee were depositedat the
same time by a holder who knew that
the drawer had not sufficient funds on
deposit to pay either check. Checks
amounting to more than the face of
either check were drawn against the de-
posit. The holderwho made the deposit
was guiltyof two offenses; convictionof
one offensewas not a bar to a prosecu-
tion for the other. Siegel v . Com ., 177
Ky .232, 197 S. W. 809.
(28 ) Two indictments - when one
barred . Two indictments were found at
same time against a railroad company
for nuisance in obstructing a road ; on
the trial of the second the company
pleaded itsacquittal under the firstas a
bar . The court held that the first trial
was only a bar as to such offensesas
were then attempted to be proved , and
that whether acts and time were covered
by firsttrialwas a question of fact upon
which evidence might be introduced. C.
& O. R. R. v. Com ., 88 Κν . 368, 10 R.
919,11 S.W. 87; Cawein v.Com .,110Ky .
273 , 22 R. 1734 , 61 S. W. 275 .
(29 ) The State has the right in the
trialof certain offensesto single out and
rely upon certain acts, and when it con-
finesitselfto particular acts the trialis
not a bar to other prosecutionsfor the
same offense committed at other times
within the year, but if the State on the
firsttrialattempts to embrace or cover
the entireperiod, then the firsttrialis a
bar to a second prosecution for the same
offense committed within the period. C.
& O. R. R. v.Com .,88 Ky .368,10 R. 919,
11 S. W. 87 ; Cawein v . Com ., 110 Ky .
273 , 22 R. 1734,61 S. W. 275 .
(30) Commonwealth is not confined to
time named in indictment, but may se-
lect and rely upon an offense committed
at any time within year. C. & O. R. R.
v.Com .,88 Ky .368,10 R. 919, 11 S.W.87; see sec. 129 and notes.
(31) Variance. When a prosecution
fails in the trial because of a variance,
as where an indictment charges false
swearing before a magistratewhen the
false swearing was before the county
judge , dismissal of firstwill not bar a
second indictment stating facts correctly.
Turner v . Com ., 19 R. 1161, 42 S. W.
1129 ; and see Gaskins v. Com ., 97 Ky .
494 , 17 R. 352, 30 S. W. 1017 ; and Code ,
sec . 178 .
§ 177. (1 ) Conviction or acquittal for
any degreea bar to prosecutionfor any
other degree. Com . v .Bright,78 Ky .238 ;
Com . v . Hawkins , 11 Bush 603 ; but see
Keddy v . Com ., 97 Ky . 784, 17 R. 536 ,
31 S. W. 730 ; Linch v . Com ., 18 R. 145,
35 S. W. 264 , and notes to sees. 262-4 .
(2) Dismissal of indictment is a final
order and can not be set aside after term
but a new indictment may be found .
Com .v .Smith ,140 Ky .580 ,131 S. W. 391.

when bar . See notes to secs. 176, 243 .

§ 180 [181] [Issues- how tried- jury trial.] Issuesof law shallbe
triedby the court. Issues of fact, in prosecutionfor offensesof which
thepunishmentislimitedto a fineof sixteendollars,shallbe triedby the
court. All otherissuesof factshallbe triedby a jury.

(2) An order of court abating an in-
dictment , although superinduced by the
mistaken beliefthat defendant was dead ,
was a termination of prosecution under

instatedon docketas a pendingprosecu-
tion against the defendant . Henry v .
Com ., 4 Bush 427.
(3) Attorney for Commonwealth must
filewritten statement of grounds of dis-
missal. Ky .Stat ., sec.123 ; but this sec-
tion does not apply when indictment is
dismissed to be resubmitted to grand
jury . Dilger v . Com ., 88 Ky . 550, 11 R.

because of when bar . See Gaskins v .

section is no bar to another prosecution
for same offense. Com . v. Elkins, 116
Ky . 603, 25 R. 485 , 76 S. W. 25 ; and see
sec. 243 and notes .

§ 181-183] [Title VITrial- Mode Of .
§ 181 [182] [Issueoflaw.) An issueof law ariseson a demurrerto
the indictment .
Cross reference: See Gregory's Ky .Crim . Law , sec.1025.
§ 182 [183] [Issueof fact.] An issueof factarisesupon a pleaof
not guilty,or of former acquittal,or conviction.
Cross reference: See Gregory'sKy .Crim.Law ,sec.1025.
§ 183 [184] [Defendanton trialforfelonymust be present- bail.] If
the indictmentbe for a felony, the defendant must be present,and shall
remaininactualcustodyduringthetrial;unlesshisbailappearpersonally
incourt,and consentthathe may remainon bail,inwhich casehe shall
be placedinactualcustodywhen thecaseisfinallysubmittedtothejury.
If he escapefrom custodyafterthe trialhas commenced ,thetrialmay
eitherbe stopped or progressto a verdict, at the discretionof the Com-
monwealth'sattorney;butjudgment shallnotbe rendereduntilthepres-
ence of the defendant is obtaind. (Felony defined, sec. 6. See further,
secs.229. 285.)
